wah
Fun
Evaluate effect of identified misstatements on audit & uncorrected misstatements, if any, on FS.
Accumulation of Identified Misstatements
Accumulate misstatements identified during audit, other than clearly trivial.
 May designate an amount below which misstatements would be clearly trivial & wouldn't be accumulated
because auditor expects accumulation of such amounts clearly wouldn't have a material effect on FS,
"Clearly trivial" is not same as "not material" [ wholly different (smaller) order of magnitude + clearly
inconsequential, individually or aggregate & judged by any criteria of size, nature or circumstances]
- When any uncertainty about whether items are clearly trivial, matter considered not clearly trivial.
Difference between factual, judgmental & projected misstatements
- Factual misstatements: Misstatements about which there is no doubt.
- Judgmental misstatements: Differences of judgments of mgt concerning $a/c$ estimates that auditor
finds unreasonable, or selection/application of $a/c$ policies that auditor finds inappropriate.
 Projected misstatements: Auditor's best estimate of misstatements in populations, involving projection
of misstatements in audit samples to entire populations from which samples were drawn.
Consideration of Identified Misstatements as Audit Progresses
Auditor shall determine whether overall audit strategy & audit plan need to be revised if:
1. Nature & circumstances of occurrence of identified misstatements indicate that other misstatements
may exist that, when aggregated with accumulated ones, could be material
OR
D. Aggregate of misstatements accumulated approaches materiality as per SA 320.
If, at auditor's request, mgt has examined & corrected detected misstatements, perform additional
audit procedures to determine whether misstatements remain.
Communication and Correction of Misstatements
 Communicate all misstatements accumulated during audit with mgt.
 Requesting mgt to correct those misstatements.
 If mgt refuses, obtain understanding of reasons & evaluate whether FS are free from material
misstatement. [Maybe an indicator of mgt bias]
Uncorrected misstatements
Misstatements auditor has accumulated during audit & not corrected.
Learn
Wah
01 Standards on Auc
Fun
Evaluating the Effect of Uncorrected Misstatements
I. Reassess Materiality
Prior to evaluating effect of uncorrected misstatements, auditor shall reassess materiality as pe
320, to confirm whether it remains appropriate in context of entity's actual financial results.
II. Check if misstatement material?
Determine whether uncorrected misstatements are material (individually or in aggregate).
Consider- (size & effect)
i) Size & nature of misstatements, both in relation to particular ABCD & FS as whole & partic
circumstances of their occurrence &
ii) Effect of uncorrected misstatements related to prior periods on relevant ABCD & FS as whole
III. Communication with TCWG:
☑
✓ Communicate with TCWG uncorrected misstatements & effect on opinion in audit report.
✓ Identify material uncorrected misstatements individually.
☑
☑
✓ Request that uncorrected misstatements be corrected.
IV. Written Representation
Request WR from mgt & TCWG that they believe effects of uncorrected misstatement are immater
to FS as whole. Summary of such items included in or attached to WR.
Documentation
a) Amount below which misstatements regarded as clearly trivial
b) All misstatements accumulated during audit & whether they have been corrected &
Auditor's conclusion as to whether uncorrected misstatements are material, individually or in aggreg
& basis for that conclusion.

SA 500: Audit Evidence
Audit evidence is info, used by auditor in arriving at conclusions on which auditor's opinion is based
✓ It includes both info. contained in accounting records & other information.
✓ Objective: To design & perform audit procedures to obtain SAAE to draw reasonable conclusions to
auditor's opinion.
✓ Info, to be used as ΑΕ
☐
Auditor shall consider relevance & reliability of info. to be used as AE.
• When using info. produced by entity, evaluate reliability, completeness, accuracy of info.
Mgt's expert: Individual or organisation possessing expertise in a field other than accounting or auc
whose work is used by entity in preparing FS.
01 Standards on Auditing
Audit evidence prepared using Mgt Expert's work
valuate competence, capabilities & objectivity (CCO) of that expert
Info. regarding CCO of a mgt's expert may come from a variety of sources, such as:
ersonal experience with previous work of expert.
iscussions with expert.
iscussions with others who are familiar with expert's work.
nowledge of expert's qualifications
ublished papers or books written by that expert.
btain understanding of work of expert
spects of mgt's expert's field relevant to auditor's understanding may include:
Learn
way
Fun
ssumptions & methods used by mgt's expert & whether they are generally accepted within expert's
eld & appropriate for financial reporting purposes.
valuate appropriateness of that expert's work as audit evidence for relevant assertion.
onsider following:
 Relevance & reasonableness of expert's findings or conclusions, consistency with other audit
evidence, & whether they have been appropriately reflected in FS
Relevance & reasonableness of significant assumptions & methods
Relevance, completeness, & accuracy of source data.
CCO → Understanding of work → Appropriateness as AE [Source data/Assumptions/Findings]
Evaluating Objectivity of Mgt Expert
range of circumstances may threaten objectivity, for eg, self-interest threats, advocacy threats,
miliarity threats, self-review threats and intimidation threats. [SSAFI]
afeguards* may reduce such threats & may be created either by
 external structures (for eg, mgt's expert's profession, legislation or regulation), or
 mgt's expert's work environment (for eg, quality control policies & procedures).
afeguards can't eliminate all threats, threats such as intimidation threats may be of less significance
an expert engaged by entity than expert employed by entity.
ecause threat to objectivity created by being employee of entity always present, expert employed by
tity can't be regarded more objective than other employees of entity.
scuss with mgt & expert any interests & relationships that may create threats to expert's objectivity
applicable safeguards & evaluate whether safeguards are adequate.
terests & relationships creating threats may include:
mancial interests, Business & personal relationships & Provision of other services.
Inconsistency or doubts over reliability of audit evidence
etermine modifications or additions to audit procedures to resolve the matter & effect on other
pects of audit.

SA 501: Audit Evidence-Specific Considerations for Selected Items
Objective of auditor is to obtain SAAE regarding:
a.
Existence & condition of inventory
b. Completeness of litigation & claims
C
Presentation & disclosure of segment information as per AFRF.
Inventory
When inventory is material to FS, obtain SAAE regarding existence and condition of inventory by
a. Attendance at physical inventory counting, unless impracticable, to:
 
Evaluate mgt's instructions & procedures for recording results of entity's physical inventory.co
Observe mgt's count procedures
• Inspect the inventory &
Perform test counts &
b. Performing audit procedures over entity's final inventory records to determine they accurately re
actual inventory count results.
Matters Relevant in Planning Attendance at Physical Inventory Counting
Locations at which inventory is held, including Materiality of inventory & RoMM at different locate
Nature of inventory.
The nature of the internal control related to inventory.
LNOR
PST
Whether adequate Procedures established & proper instructions issued for inventory counting.
Whether entity maintains a Perpetual inventory system.
Romm related to inventory.
• Stages of completion of work inprogress.
• Timing of physical inventory counting.
I.
Whether assistance of an auditor's expert is needed.
Procedures in Special Circumstances
Physical inventory count at date other than date of FS:
Auditor perform audit procedures to obtain audit evidence about whether changes in inventory bete
count date & date of FS are properly recorded.
Matters relevant when such designing audit procedures include:
1. Whether perpetual inventory records are properly adjusted.
2. Reliability of entity's perpetual inventory records.
3. Reasons for significant differences between physical count & perpetual records.

 Standards on Auditing
Auditor unable to attend inventory count:
Learn
With
Fun
If auditor unable to attend physical inventory counting due to unforeseen circumstances, observe
physical counts on alternative date
Perform audit procedures to assess whether changes in inventory between date of physical count &
period end are correctly recorded.
Also verify treatment given for discrepancies noticed during physical count.
Also ensure appropriate cut off procedures followed by mgt.
Also get mgt's written representation on
a. completeness of info provided regarding inventory, and
b. assurance w.r.t adherence to laid down procedures for physical inventory count.
If attendance is impracticable:
This may be due to location & nature of inventory. Eg. Location pose threat to Auditor
General inconvenience, not sufficient to support decision by auditor that attendance is impracticable.
As per SA 200, matter of difficulty, time, or cost is not a valid basis to omit an audit procedure for
which no alternative available or settle for less than persuasive Audit Evidence.
If attendance impracticable, perform alternative audit procedures.
Eg, inspection of documentation of subsequent sale of inventory purchased prior to inventory counting.
If not possible to obtain SAAE, modify opinion as per SA 705.
Inventory in custody of $3^{rd}$ Party:
When inventory under custody of $3^{rd}$ party is material to FS, obtain SAAE regarding existence &
condition of inventory by performing one or both of following:
Request confirmation from $3^{rd}$ party as to quantities & condition of inventory held on behalf of entity.
Perform inspection or other audit procedures appropriate in circumstances.
If doubt over integrity & objectivity of $3^{rd}$ party, perform other audit procedures:
Attending or arranging another auditor to attend $3^{rd}$ party's physical counting of inventory, if practicable.
Obtaining another auditor's report or service auditor's report on adequacy of $3^{rd}$
party's internal control
that inventory is properly counted and adequately safeguarded.
Inspecting documentation regarding inventory held by $3^{rd}$ parties, for eg, warehouse receipts.
Requesting confirmation from other parties when inventory has been pledged as collateral.
Note: Also inspect record of goods sent by tracing it to challans, e-ways bills etc.
Litigation & Claims (L&Cs)
Identify L&Cs through following procedures:
Inquiry of mgt & others within entity, including in-house legal counsel
Reviewing minutes of meetings of TCWG & correspondence $b/w$ entity & external legal counsel
Reviewing legal expense $a/c$

Direct Communication with External legal counsel (ELC)
01 Standards on A
➤ If auditor assesses ROMM regarding litigation or claims  seek direct communication with ent
➤ Auditor shall do so through letter of inquiry, prepared by mgt & sent by auditor, requesting ent
to communicate directly with auditor.
If ELC will not respond to letter of general inquiry, use letter of specific inquiry which includes
a. A list of litigation & claim
b. Where available, mgt's assessment of outcome of each identified L/Cs & estimate of
implications, including costs involved &
c. Request that ELC confirm reasonableness of mgt's assessments & provide further info. i
incomplete or incorrect.
Meeting ELC: Auditor may meet entity's ELC to discuss likely outcome of litigation or claims.
This may be the case where, matter is a significant risk, complex or there's disagreement betu
& entity's ELC. Such meetings require mgt's permission & held with a representative of mgt in att
➤ If law, regulation or respective legal professional body prohibits entity's ELC from commu
directly with auditor, perform alternative audit procedures.
If mgt refuses or ELC refuses to respond &
Auditor unable to obtain SAAE by performing alternative audit procedures,
Modify opinion as per SA 705
Written Representation from mgt & TCWG that all L&Cs disclosed to auditor & accounted & disc
per AFRF
Segment Information
Auditor shall obtain SAAE about presentation & disclosure of segment info as per applicable FF
a. Obtaining understanding of methods used by Mgt in determining segment information
b. Performing analytical procedures or other audit procedures appropriate in circumstances.
Example of matters' relevant when understanding mgt methods in determining segment info. in
i) Sales, transfers & charges between segments & elimination of inter-segment amounts.
ii) Comparisons with budgets & other expected results, for eg, operating profits as %age of sales.
iii) Allocation of assets & costs among segments..
iv) Consistency with prior periods & adequacy of disclosures w.r.t inconsistencies.

SA 505: External Confirmations
xternal confirmation: Audit evidence obtained as a direct written response to auditor from a $3^{rd}$ party
Confirming party), in paper form, or by electronic or other medium.
External Confirmation Procedures
When using external confirmation procedures, auditor shall maintain control over external confirmation
equests, including:
etermining info. to be confirmed or requested
electing appropriate confirming party
esigning confirmation requests, including determining that requests are properly addressed & contain
eturn info. for responses to be sent directly to auditor
ending the requests, including follow-up requests when applicable, to confirming party.
actors to consider when designing confirmation requests include: (Inter) Imp
ayout & presentation of confirmation request.
ssertions being addressed.
-ior experience on the audit or similar engg.
pecific identified ROMM, including fraud risks
LAPRMM
related parties
gt's authorisation or encouragement to confirming parties to respond to auditor. Confirming parties
ay be willing to respond to a confirmation request containing mgt's authorisation.
ethod of communication (for eg, in paper form, or by electronic or other medium).
bility of intended confirming party to confirm or provide requested info. (for eg, individual invoice
mount versus total balance)
Positive Confirmation Requests (PCR)
CR asks confirming party to reply in all cases, either by indicating agreement or providing info.
esponse to PCR expected to provide reliable audit evidence.
mere is risk that confirming party may reply without verifying if info. is correct.
uditor may reduce this risk by not stating amt on confirmation request & ask confirming party to fill
mount or furnish other info.
 other hand, use of this type of "blank" confirmation request may result in lower response rates
ecause additional effort required by confirming parties.
Negative Confirmations Request (NCR)
equest that confirming party respond directly to auditor only if confirming party disagrees with info.
-ovided in request. NCR provide less persuasive evidence than positive confirmations.
uditor shall not use NCR as sole substantive audit procedure to address assessed ROMM at assertion
vel unless all of following are present:

01 Standards on Auditing
Learn
With
Fun
SA 505: External Confirmations
External confirmation: Audit evidence obtained as a direct written response to auditor from a 3rd party
(confirming party), in paper form, or by electronic or other medium.
External Confirmation Procedures
When using external confirmation procedures, auditor shall maintain control over external confirmation
requests, including:
Determining info. to be confirmed or requested
Selecting appropriate confirming party
Designing confirmation requests, including determining that requests are properly addressed & contain
return info. for responses to be sent directly to auditor
Sending the requests, including follow-up requests when applicable, to confirming party.
Factors to consider when designing confirmation requests include: (Inter) Imp
Layout & presentation of confirmation request.
Assertions being addressed.
Prior experience on the audit or similar engg.
LAPRMM
Specific identified ROMM, including fraud risks related parties
Mgt's authorisation or encouragement to confirming parties to respond to auditor. Confirming parties
may be willing to respond to a confirmation request containing mgt's authorisation.
Method of communication (for eg, in paper form, or by electronic or other medium).
Ability of intended confirming party to confirm or provide requested info. (for eg, individual invoice
amount versus total balance)
Positive Confirmation Requests (PCR)
PCR asks confirming party to reply in all cases, either by indicating agreement or providing info.
Response to PCR expected to provide reliable audit evidence.
There is risk that confirming party may reply without verifying if info. is correct.
Auditor may reduce this risk by not stating amt on confirmation request & ask confirming party to fill
amount or furnish other info.
On other hand, use of this type of "blank" confirmation request may result in lower response rates
because additional effort required by confirming parties.
Negative Confirmations Request (NCR)
Request that confirming party respond directly to auditor only if confirming party disagrees with info.
provided in request. NCR provide less persuasive evidence than positive confirmations.
Auditor shall not use NCR as sole substantive audit procedure to address assessed ROMM at assertion
level unless all of following are present:
Learn
Wah
Fun
01 Standards on Audit
a Auditor has assessed ROMM as low & obtained SAAE regarding operating effectiveness of controls
b. Population comprises large no. of small, homogeneous, account balances, transactions or conditions
C.
A very low exception rate is expected &
d. Auditor is not aware of circumstances that would cause recipients to disregard such requests.
No response in case of Negative confirmation:
Failure to receive response doesn't indicate receipt by intended confirming party of confirmati
request or verification of accuracy of info contained in request.
Failure of confirming party to respond provides significantly less persuasive audit evidence than PCR
Also, confirming parties more likely to respond with disagreement when info. requested is not in the
favour & less likely to respond otherwise.
Non-Response
Failure of confirming party to respond, or fully respond, to PCR, or a confirmation request returne
undelivered. In such case, auditor shall perform alternate audit procedures.
Examples of alternative audit procedures auditor may perform include:
$A/c$ receivable balances examining specific subsequent cash receipts, shipping documentation,
sales near period-end.
$A/c$ payable balances
examining subsequent cash disbursements or correspondence from 3-
parties, & other records, such as goods received notes.
Exception
A response that indicates a difference between info. requested to be confirmed, or contained in entity's
records, & info. provided by confirming party.
Auditor shall investigate exceptions to determine whether or not they are indicative of misstatement
Exception needs to be assessed to entire population after analysing reason for difference.
Auditor's evaluation, when considered with other procedures may assist in concluding whether SAAB
been obtained or performing FAP is necessary, as per SA 330.
Mgt's Refusal to Allow the Auditor to Send a Confirmation Request
If mgt refuses to allow auditor to send a confirmation request, auditor shall:
a. Inquire mgt's reasons for refusal & seek audit evidence of their validity & reasonableness
b. Evaluate implications of mgt's refusal on auditor's assessment of ROMM, including risk of fraud &
NTE of other audit procedures &
C. Perform alternative audit procedures to obtain relevant & reliable audit evidence.
✓ If auditor concludes mgt's refusal is unreasonable or unable to obtain relevant & reliable audit
✓
evidence from alternative audit procedures, communicate with TCWG as per SA 260,
Determine implications for audit & auditor's opinion as per SA 705.

Failure of confirming party to respond provides significantly less persuasive audit evidence than Po
Also, confirming parties more likely to respond with disagreement when info. requested is not in th

Non-Response
Failure of confirming party to respond, or fully respond, to PCR, or a confirmation request retur

Examples of alternative audit procedures auditor may perform include:
•
$A/c$ receivable balances examining specific subsequent cash receipts, shipping documentation

parties, & other records, such as goods received notes.
Exception
A response that indicates a difference between info. requested to be confirmed, or contained in entit
records, & info. provided by confirming party.
Auditor shall investigate exceptions to determine whether or not they are indicative of misstatemen
Exception needs to be assessed to entire population after analysing reason for difference.
Auditor's evaluation, when considered with other procedures may assist in concluding whether SAP
been obtained or performing FAP is necessary, as per SA 330.
Mgt's Refusal to Allow the Auditor to Send a Confirmation Request
If mgt refuses to allow auditor to send a confirmation request, auditor shall:
. Inquire mgt's reasons for refusal & seek audit evidence of their validity & reasonableness
Evaluate implications of mgt's refusal on auditor's assessment of ROMM, including risk of fraud &
NTE of other audit procedures &
Perform alternative audit procedures to obtain relevant & reliable audit evidence.
✓ If auditor concludes mgt's refusal is unreasonable or unable to obtain relevant & reliable aud
evidence from alternative audit procedures, communicate with TCWG as per SA 260.
✓ Determine implications for audit & auditor's opinion as per SA 705.
 Standards on Auditing
Learn
Wah
Fun
Reliability of Response
SA 505 states if auditor determines response to confirmation request not reliable, evaluate
implications on RoMM, including risk of fraud & NTE of other audit procedures.
As per SA 500, even when audit evidence obtained from external sources, circumstances may exist
that affect its reliability. All responses carry risk of interception, alteration or fraud. Such risk exists
regardless of whether response is obtained in paper form, or by electronic or other medium.
Factors that may indicate doubts about reliability of a response include that it:
Was received by auditor indirectly OR appeared not to come from originally intended confirming party
Unreliable responses may indicate a fraud risk factor that requires evaluation.
SA 510: Initial Audit Engagements - Opening Balances
Initial audit engg.: Engg. in which FS for prior period, not audited or audited by a predecessor auditor
Opening balances: $A/c$ balances that exist at beginning of period. Also include disclosures that existe
at beginning of period, such as contingencies & commitments.
Objective
To obtain SAAE about whether:
Opening balances contain misstatements that materially affect current period's FS
Appropriate $a/c$ policies reflected in opening balances been consistently applied in current period's F
or changes properly accounted, presented & disclosed as per AFRF.
Audit Procedures: Opening Balances
Read most recent financial statements & audit report.
Obtain SAAE about whether opening balances contain material misstatements by:
Determining whether prior period's closing balances correctly brought forward or any adjustme
disclosed as prior period items in current year's P&L
Determining whether opening balances reflect application of appropriate
Performing one or more of following audit procedures:
i) Where prior year FS were audited, perusing copies of audited FS
$a/c$
policies
ii) Evaluating whether current period audit procedures provide evidence of opening balances or
iii) Performing specific audit procedures to obtain evidence regarding opening balances.
If auditor obtains audit evidence that opening balances contain misstatements that could materi
affect current period's FS, perform additional audit procedures to determine effect.
If auditor concludes misstatements exist in current period's FS, communicate with mgt & TCWG.
141
01 Standards on Auditing
Lear
Wille
Fun
Reliability of Response
SA 505 states if auditor determines response to confirmation request not reliable, evaluate
implications on RoMM, including risk of fraud & NTE of other audit procedures.
As per SA 500, even when audit evidence obtained from external sources, circumstances may exis-
that affect its reliability. All responses carry risk of interception, alteration or fraud. Such risk exists
regardless of whether response is obtained in paper form, or by electronic or other medium.
Factors that may indicate doubts about reliability of a response include that it:
Was received by auditor indirectly OR appeared not to come from originally intended confirming party
Unreliable responses may indicate a fraud risk factor that requires evaluation.
SA 510: Initial Audit Engagements - Opening Balances
Initial audit engg.: Engg. in which FS for prior period, not audited or audited by a predecessor auditor
Opening balances: $A/c$ balances that exist at beginning of period. Also include disclosures that existe
at beginning of period, such as contingencies & commitments.
Objective
To obtain SAAE about whether:
 Opening balances contain misstatements that materially affect current period's FS
) Appropriate $a/c$ policies reflected in opening balances been consistently applied in current period's F
or changes properly accounted, presented & disclosed as per AFRF.
Audit Procedures: Opening Balances
Read most recent financial statements & audit report.
Obtain SAAE about whether opening balances contain material misstatements by:
) Determining whether prior period's closing balances correctly brought forward or any adjustme
disclosed as prior period items in current year's P&L
) Determining whether opening balances reflect application of appropriate $a/c$ policies
) Performing one or more of following audit procedures:
i) Where prior year FS were audited, perusing copies of audited FS
ii) Evaluating whether current period audit procedures provide evidence of opening balances or
iii) Performing specific audit procedures to obtain evidence regarding opening balances.
 If auditor obtains audit evidence that opening balances contain misstatements that could materi
affect current period's FS, perform additional audit procedures to determine effect.
 If auditor concludes misstatements exist in current period's FS, communicate with mgt & TCWG.

Fun
ards on Aud
1. Debtors/Creditors: Collection (payment) of opening $a/c$ receivable $(a/c$ payable) during current per
Opening current assets & liabilities audit evidence obtained during current audit procedurer
2. Inventories: Current period's audit procedures on closing inventory balance provide little audit evid
regarding opening inventory.
Additional audit procedures may be necessary, & one or more of following may provide SAAE:
 Observing a current physical inventory count & reconciling to opening inventory quantities.
• Performing audit procedures on valuation of opening inventory items.
 Performing audit procedures on gross profit & cut-off.
Consistency of $A/c$ Policies
Obtain SAAE that $A/c$ policies been consistently applied &
any changes been properly accounted & adequately presented & disclosed as per AFRF.
If not
Qualified/Adverse opinion as per SA 705
Conclusion & Reporting
Unable to obtain SAAE  Qualify/Disclaimer of opinion as per SA 705
Opening balance contain misstatement that materially affects current period FS & not pro
accounted & disclosed Qualified/Adverse Opinion as per SA 705
Modification to opinion in predecessor audit report: If relevant & material to current FS, modify c
period audit report.

Objectives of Auditor
To design & perform An Pro. near end of audit for forming
overall conclusion as to whether FS are consistent with
auditor's understanding of entity.
 Substantive Analytical Procedures (SAP)

 Standards on Auditing
Learn
wah
Fun
Evaluate reliability of data from which auditor's expectation of recorded amounts or ratios is developed,
taking account of source, comparability, and nature and relevance of information available, and controls
over preparation
Develop expectation of recorded amounts or ratios and evaluate whether expectation is sufficiently
precise to identify misstatement that may cause F.S, to be materially misstated and
Determine amount of difference of recorded amounts from expected values that is acceptable without
further investigation.
Suitability >>> Reliability >> Expectation >>> Investigation
Analytical Procedures that assist when Forming Overall Conclusion
Perform analytical procedures near end of audit when forming overall conclusion whether FS are
consistent with auditor's understanding of entity.
May identify a previously unrecognised RoMM.
SA 315 requires auditor to revise RoMM & modify FAP.
Analytical procedures performed above may be similar to risk assessment procedures.
Investigating Results of Analytical Procedures
If auditor identifies fluctuations or relationships inconsistent with other relevant info or differ from
expected values by significant amt, auditor shall investigate such differences by:
■) Inquiring of mgt & obtaining appropriate audit evidence relevant to mgt's responses
Audit evidence relevant to mgt's responses may be obtained by evaluating responses considering
understanding of entity & environment & other audit evidence obtained during audit.
 ) Performing other audit procedures when,
✓ mgt unable to provide explanation or
✓ explanation with audit evidence obtained, not considered adequate..
SA 530 "Audit Sampling"
Objective: To provide reasonable basis to draw conclusions about population.
Audit sampling (sampling):
Application of audit procedures to less than 100% items within a population such that all sampling units
have a chance of selection to provide a reasonable basis to draw conclusions about entire population.
Sampling risk: Risk that auditor's conclusion based on sample may be different from conclusion if entire
population were subjected to same audit procedure.
Types of Sampling Risks?
Sampling risk can lead to 2 types of erroneous conclusions:
i) In case of TOCs, controls are more effective than actual OR in case of TODs, material misstatement
does not exist when it does. Affects audit effectiveness & lead to inappropriate audit opinion.

 ) Determine amount of difference of recorded amounts from expected values that is acceptable without

 ) Inquiring of mgt & obtaining appropriate audit evidence relevant to mgt's responses

b) Performing other audit procedures when,

SA 530 "Audit Sampling"
Objective: To provide reasonable basis to draw conclusions about population.
Audit sampling (sampling):
Application of audit procedures to less than 100% items within a population such that all sampling unit
have a chance of selection to provide a reasonable basis to draw conclusions about entire population.
Sampling risk: Risk that auditor's conclusion based on sample may be different from conclusion if entir
population were subjected to same audit procedure.
Types of Sampling Risks?
Sampling risk can lead to 2 types of erroneous conclusions:
i) In case of TOCs, controls are more effective than actual OR in case of TODs, material misstatemer
does not exist when it does Affects audit effectiveness & lead to inappropriate audit opinion.
Learn
いさん
01 Standards on Auditin
Fun
 ii) In case of TOCs, controls are less effective than they actually are, OR in case of TODS, mater
misstatement exists when in fact it does not. Affects audit efficiency & lead to additional work-
establish that initial conclusions were incorrect.
Non-Sampling Risk: Risk of erroneous conclusion for any reason not related to sampling risk.
Note: Detection risk comprises sampling & non-sampling risk.
Tolerable misstatement [TM]
Monetary amt set by auditor for which auditor seeks to obtain appropriate level of assurance that th
amount isn't exceeded by actual misstatement in population.
Its application of performance materiality, to a sampling procedure.
It may be same amount or amount lower than performance materiality.
Sample Design, Size & Selection of Items for Testing
When designing audit sample, consider purpose of audit & characteristics of population.
 Determine sample size sufficient to reduce sampling risk to acceptably low level.
Select items in a way that each sampling unit in population has chance of selection.
☐
Stratification & Value-Weighted Selection (Inter)
Stratification: Audit efficiency may be improved if auditor stratifies a population by dividing it int
discrete sub-populations (Stratum) having identifying characteristics.
How?
When performing TODs, population often stratified by monetary value. This allows greater effor
directed to large value items, as these items likely to be overstated.
Similarly, population may be stratified as per particular characteristic that indicates higher RoMM,
Eg, when testing provision for doubtful $a/c$ in valuation of debtors, balances may be stratified by age.
Projection:
Results of samples drawn under each sub population are projected to that respective stratum.
Projected misstatements of each stratum combined together to consider possible effect
misstatement in account balances & class of transactions.
Value-Weighted Selection: When performing TODs it may be efficient to identify sampling unit
Individual monetary units that make up population. Selecting specific monetary units from population.
Principal methods are as follows:
Sample Selection Methods (Inter)
Random selection (applied through random number generators, for eg, random number tables).
• Simple Random Sampling: Whole population has equal chance of selection
*
• Stratified Sampling: Dividing population in few groups called strata & taking samples from them.

 Standards on Auditing
interval.
Learn
wah
Fun
Systematic selection, in which no, of sampling units in population divided by sample size to give sampling
Eg, there are 5000 items & I want 100 samples, interval=5000/100=50, & having determined starting
point within first 50 (randomly), each 50th sampling unit thereafter is selected.
Monetary Unit Sampling is a type of value-weighted selection in which sample size, selection & evaluation
results in a conclusion in monetary amounts.
Haphazard selection, select sample without following structured technique.
Block selection Selection of block(s) of contiguous items within population, Can't be used in audit sampling.
Performing audit procedures
Perform audit procedures, appropriate to purpose, on each item selected.
If audit procedure isn't applicable to selected item, auditor apply procedure on replacement item.
If unable to apply designed audit procedures, or alternative procedures, to selected item, treat that
item as deviation from prescribed control, in case of TOCs, or misstatement, in case of TODS.
Nature & Cause of Deviations & Misstatements
Investigate nature & cause of deviations or misstatements identified & evaluate possible effect on audit
procedures & other areas of audit.
In extremely rare circumstances when auditor considers a misstatement/deviation to be anomaly, obtain
high degree of certainty that such misstatement or deviation is not representative of population.
Perform additional audit procedures to obtain SAAE that misstatement/deviation doesn't affect
remainder of population.
Projecting Misstatements
To obtain broad view of scale of misstatement but not sufficient to determine amount to be recorded.
When misstatement considered anomaly, its excluded when projecting misstatements to population.
However, such misstatement, if uncorrected, considered in addition to projected misstatement.
For TOD, project misstatements to population.
Auditor shall evaluate-
) The results of sample and
Evaluating Results of Audit Sampling
 ) Whether use of audit sampling has provided reasonable basis for conclusions about the population tested.
If auditor concludes audit sampling hasn't provided reasonable basis for conclusions about population
tested, auditor may:
i. request mgt to investigate misstatements, potential for further misstatements & make adjustments OR
i, tailor NTE of further audit procedures to best achieve required assurance.
Eg. in TOCs, might extend sample size, test alternative control or modify related substantive procedures.

01 Standards on Auditing
Read
Mgt Bias
Learn
with
Fun
FRFs require neutrality i.e. freedom from bias. A $A/c$ estimates can be influenced by mgt judgment.
May involve mgt bias. Unintentional & intentional mgt bias are inherent in making $a/c$ estimate.
Mgt bias is difficult to detect at a $a/c$ level. May be identified only when considered in aggregate of
Mgt bias identified during previous audit influence current period risk identification & assessmentRommt
groups of $a/c$ estimates or all $a/c$ estimates or when observed over a no. of accounting periods.
Although some mgt bias is inherent in a/c estimates, there may be no intention to mislead users of FS.
However, when there is intention to mislead, management bias is fraudulent in nature.
Measurement objective of $a/c$ estimates
It can vary depending on AFRF & financial item.
litigation
For some $a/c$ estimates it is to forecast outcome of transactions, events or conditions.
For others, including FV $a/c$ estimates, it is different & expressed in terms of value of FS item based
on conditions on measurement date, such as estimated market price for asset or liability.
For eg, AFRF may require FV measurement based on assumed hypothetical current transaction between
knowledgeable, willing parties (referred as "marketplace participants" or equivalent) in an arm's length
transaction, rather than the settlement of a transaction at some past or future date.
Difference between outcome of $a/c$ estimate & amount recognised/disclosed in FS does not necessarily
represent a misstatement. This is for FV $a/c$ estimates, as any outcome is affected by events or
conditions subsequent to date at which its measured for FS.
To obtain SAAE whether as per AFRF:
Objective of SA 540
a/c estimates, including FV $a/c$ estimates in FS are reasonable &
related disclosures in FS are adequate.
Risk Assessment Procedures & Related Activities for $A/c$ estimates
In identifying & assessing RoMM as per SA 315, evaluate degree of estimation uncertainty.
Determine whether any $a/c$ estimate having high estimation uncertainty give rise to significant risk.
Obtain understanding of
✓ Requirements of FRF
✓ How mgt identifies those transactions, events or conditions that give rise to $a/c$ estimates by
making inquiries
Estimation making process of mgt:
MACEC

Although some mgt bias is inherent in $a/c$ estimates, there may be no intention to mislead users of FS.

To obtain SAAE whether as per AFRF:
Objective of SA 540
■) a/c estimates, including FV $a/c$ estimates in FS are reasonable &
 ) related disclosures in FS are adequate.
Risk Assessment Procedures & Related Activities for $A/c$ estimates
■) In identifying & assessing RoMM as per SA 315, evaluate degree of estimation uncertainty.
 ) Determine whether any $a/c$ estimate having high estimation uncertainty give rise to significant risk.

Estimation making process of mgt:
Method used in making $a/c$ estimate
MACEC
Assumptions underlying $a/c$ estimates
Whether any change from prior period in methods for making $a/c$ estimates & if so, why
Whether mat has used an expert
Relevant controls
Whether and, if so, how mgt has assessed effect of estimation uncertainty.
Learn
With
01 Standards on Aude
Fun
•
Inquiries from Management
Inquiries of mgt about changes in circumstances may include, for eg, inquiries about whether:
Entity has engaged in new types of transactions that may give rise to $a/c$ estimates.
✓ Terms of transactions that gave rise to $a/c$ estimates that have changed.
✓ $A/c$ policies of $a/c$ estimates have changed, as a result of changes in AFRF or otherwise.
✓ Regulatory or other changes $o/s$ mgt control that may require mgt to revise or make new $a/c$ est.
☑ New conditions or events have occurred that give rise to need for new or revised $a/c$ estimates.
Auditor may identify transactions, events & conditions that give rise to need for $a/c$ estimates t
mgt failed to identify. As per SA 315 it may be significant deficiency in internal control.
Review of Outcome of Accounting Estimates
Auditor shall review outcome of prior period $a/c$ estimates or their subsequent re-estimation.
Nature & extent of review considers
✓ nature of $a/c$ estimates &
✓ whether review relevant to identify & assess ROMM of $a/c$ estimates made in current period F
However, review is not intended to question judgments made in prior periods. Outcome of $a/c$ estimo
will often differ from $a/c$ estimate recognised in prior period F.S.
By performing RAP to identify & understand reasons for such differences, auditor may obtain:
✓ Info. about effectiveness of mgt's prior period estimation process, to judge effectiveness of mo
current process.
✓ Audit evidence of re-estimation, in current period, of prior period $a/c$ estimates.
✓ Audit evidence of matters, such as estimation uncertainty, that may require disclosure in FS.
• May assist in identifying circumstances of possible mgt bias. Auditor's professional scepticism assis
in identifying such circumstances & determining NTE of further audit procedures.
Note: For $a/c$ estimates with significant risks, obtain SAAE whether following as per AFRF:
a) mgt's decision to recognise, or to not recognise, $a/c$ estimates in FS &
b) selected measurement basis for $a/c$ estimates.
Auditor's Response to Assessed RoMM [SA 330]
Based on assessed RoMM, determine: -
a) Whether mat has appropriately applied requirements of AFRF to $a/c$ estimate &
b) Whether methods are appropriate & applied consistently &
c) Changes in $a/c$ estimates or method from prior period are appropriate.
In responding to assessed RoMM, as per SA 330, undertake one or more of following:
a) Check whether events occurring up to date of auditor's report provide audit evidence of $a/c$ estima
b) Test how mgt made $a/c$ estimate & data on which it is based.

Disclosures in F.S.

Presentation of FS as per AFRF includes adequate disclosure that may include:
Assumptions used.
Basis for selection of estimate.
Method, including any applicable model.
Changes in method from prior period & subsequent effect.
Sources & implication of estimation uncertainty.
For $a/c$ estimate having significant risk, even when disclosures are as per AFRF, auditor may conclude
disclosure of estimation uncertainty is inadequate.

SA 550: Related Parties (RP)
Objectives of Auditor
a) Obtain understanding of RP relationships & transactions to:
i. Recognise fraud risk factors, arising from such transactions &
ii. Conclude whether FS:
☑
☑
Achieve true and fair presentation (for fair presentation frameworks) or
Are not misleading (for compliance frameworks)
b) Obtain SAAE whether RP relationships & transactions identified, accounted & disclosed in FS
Related party - Party that is either:
i) RP as defined in FRF or
ii) Where FRF establishes minimal or no related party requirements:
a) Person or other entity that has control or significant influence, over reporting entity
b) Another entity over which reporting entity has control or significant influence or
c) Another entity that is under common control with reporting entity through having:
Common controlling ownership or Owners who are close family members or Common key mgt
Exception: Entities under common control by a state (i.e. national, regional or local govt) not considere
related unless they engage in significant transactions or share significant resources with one another
But as per Ind AS 24, entity required to disclose:
1. Name of govt & its relationship with reporting entity (i.e. control, joint control or significant influence
2. Following info.:
i. nature & amount of each individual significant transaction &
ii. other transactions collectively, but not individually, significant.
Requirements of this SA (Read)
1. Risk Assessment procedures & related activities.
2. Understanding Entity's RP relationships and transactions.
3. Maintaining alertness for RP info. when reviewing records or documents.
4. Sharing RP info. with Engg. team.
5. Identification & assessment of RoMM associated with RP transactions & relationships.
5. Responses to RoMM associated with RP relationships & transactions.
Engagement Team Discussion
Matters that may be addressed in discussion by engg. team may include:
Nature & extent of entity's relationship & transactions with Related Parties
Records or documents that may indicate existence of RP relationships or transactions

Facts that become known to auditor after date of Audit report (AR) but before FS are issued
No obligation to perform any procedure regarding FS after date of AR.

Provide new or amended AR that includes EOM/OM para that conveys audit procedures on subsequent
events restricted to amendments in FS

SA 570: Going Concern (GC)
Going Concern Basis of Accounting
FS are prepared on assumption that entity will continue operations for foreseeable future.
General purpose FS are prepared using GC basis of accounting, unless mgt intends to liquidate ent
cease operations or has no other realistic alternative.
Special purpose FS may or may not be prepared as per GC basis of accounting.
When GC basis is appropriate assets & liabilities are recorded on basis that entity will be ab
realize its assets & discharge liabilities in normal course of business.
If GC not appropriate → FS prepared using liquidation basis.
Responsibilities of mgt
a) To assess entity's ability to continue as GC.
b) To make appropriate disclosures of GC in the FS.
Auditor's Responsibilities
• Obtain SAAE & conclude on, appropriateness of mgt's use of GC basis of accounting
To conclude whether a material uncertainty exists about entity's ability to continue as a GC.
However, auditor cannot guarantee entity's ability to continue as a GC.
Risk Assessment Procedures & Related Activities
When performing RAP as per SA 315, consider whether events or conditions exist that may
significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as a G.C.
In doing so, determine whether mat has already performed a preliminary assessment of entity's a
to continue as a G.C. &
If assessment performed  discuss the assessment with mgt and determine whether mat has ident
events or conditions that, individually or collectively, cast significant doubt on entity's ability to con
as going concern &, if so, mgt's plans to address them OR

✓ If Not, then Extend procedures to date of new AR, dated not before approval of amended FS
✓ Provide a new AR on amended FS
In New or amended AR include EOM/OM para referring to note in FS discussing reasons fo
amendment in FS & AR
d. If mgt doesn't amend FS & inform users, notify mgt & TCWG that auditor seek to prevent futur
reliance on AR
e.

on AR (obtain legal advice)
SA 570: Going Concern (GC)
Going Concern Basis of Accounting
FS are prepared on assumption that entity will continue operations for foreseeable future.
• General purpose FS are prepared using GC basis of accounting, unless mgt intends to liquidate entity
cease operations or has no other realistic alternative.
Special purpose FS may or may not be prepared as per GC basis of accounting.
When GC basis is appropriate  assets & liabilities are recorded on basis that entity will be able
realize its assets & discharge liabilities in normal course of business.
• If GC not appropriate → FS prepared using liquidation basis.
Responsibilities of mgt
a) To assess entity's ability to continue as GC.
b) To make appropriate disclosures of GC in the FS.
Auditor's Responsibilities
Obtain SAAE & conclude on, appropriateness of mgt's use of GC basis of accounting
To conclude whether a material uncertainty exists about entity's ability to continue as a GC.
However, auditor cannot guarantee entity's ability to continue as a GC.
Risk Assessment Procedures & Related Activities
When performing RAP as per SA 315, consider whether events or conditions exist that may cas
significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as a G.C.
In doing so, determine whether mat has already performed a preliminary assessment of entity's abilit
to continue as a G.C. &
a) If assessment performed → discuss the assessment with mgt and determine whether mat has identifie
events or conditions that, individually or collectively, cast significant doubt on entity's ability to continue
as going concern &, if so, mgt's plans to address them OR

Evaluating mgt assessment of GC (Inter)
Auditor shall evaluate mgt's assessment of entity's ability to continue as GC.
-Cover same period as used by mgt to make its assessment as required by applicable FRF.
- If mgt's assessment covers less than 12 months from date of FS, request mgt to extend assessment
period to at least 12 months.
In evaluating mgt's assessment, consider whether it includes all relevant info of which auditor is aware.
Additional Audit Procedures When Events or Conditions Are Identified
If events & conditions identified which cast significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as GC, obtain
SAAE to determine whether material uncertainty exists including mitigating factors.
. Where mgt has not yet performed GC assessment, requesting mgt to make its assessment.
5. Evaluating mgt's plans for future actions, whether outcome of these plans likely to improve situation &
whether plans are feasible.
. Where entity has prepared cash flow forecast & analysis of forecast is a significant factor in considering
future outcome of events or conditions:
i. Evaluating reliability of underlying data generated to prepare forecast &
ii. Determining whether there is adequate support for assumptions underlying forecast.
d. Considering whether any additional facts or info available since date mgt made its assessment.
2. Requesting WRs from mgt & TCWG, regarding plans & their feasibility.
Request for assessment >> Plan (Improve + Feasible) >> Cash flow forecast (Data + Assumptions) >>
Add. Facts/Info >> WR (Plans + feasible)
Additional procedures:
 Analysing & discussing cash flow, profit and other relevant forecasts with mgt.
Analysing & discussing entity's latest available interim FS.
 Reading terms of debentures & loan agreements & determining any breach.
 Reading minutes of meetings of shareholders, TCWG & committees w.r.t financing difficulties.
 Inquiring of entity's legal counsel regarding existence of litigation & claims & reasonableness of mgt's
assessments of outcome & estimate of financial implications.
Confirming existence, legality & enforceability of arrangements to provide or maintain financial support
with related & 3rd parties & assessing financial ability of such parties to provide additional funds.
 Evaluating entity's plans to deal with unfilled customer orders.

Adequacy of Disclosures
When Events or Conditions Identified & Material Uncertainty Exists
Determine whether FS:
a. Adequately disclose principal events or conditions that may cast significant doubt on entity's abilit
continue as a GC & mgt's plans to deal with these events or conditions &
b. Disclose clearly there's material uncertainty related to events or conditions that may cast significe
doubt on entity's ability to continue as a GC & it may be unable to realize its assets & discharge
liabilities in normal course of business.
Disclosures may include:
i) Mgt's evaluation of significance of events or conditions for entity's ability to meet its obligations or
ii) Significant judgements by mgt as a part of its GC assessment
iii) Magnitude of potential impact of principal events or conditions, & likelihood & timing of occurrence.
When Events or Conditions Identified but No Material Uncertainty Exists
Evaluate whether FS provide adequate disclosures about these events or conditions.
Implications for Reporting
Use of GC is Inappropriate: Express adverse opinion (even if mgt disclose GC is inappropriate in the FS)

section under heading "Material Uncertainty Related to Going Concern" to:
Draw attention to note in FS that discloses the matters &
State that M.U. exists that may cast significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as a G.C. & auditor's
opinion isn't modified in respect of the matter.

Material Uncertainty Related to Going Concern
We draw attention to Note 10 in FS, which indicates that outcome of a litigation on account of labour
laws is pending in case of Co. during the year 31 March, 2024, As stated in Note 11, this event or
condition, indicate that a material uncertainty exists that may cast significant doubt on Co's ability to
continue as a GC. Our opinion is not modified in respect of this matter.
Note: SA 701 explains that a material uncertainty related to events or conditions that may cast
significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as a GC is, by its nature, a KAM.
Adequate Disclosure of M.U. Not Made in FS
a) Express Qualified or Adverse opinion as per SA 705 &
b) In Basis for Qualified (Adverse) Opinion section of audit report, state that M.U. exists that may cast
significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as GC & FS don't adequately disclose this matter.
Mgt Unwilling to Make/Extend Its Assessment
Consider implications for auditor's report (Qualified/Disclaimer of opinion)
Communication with TCWG
Events or conditions identified that may cast significant doubt on entity's ability to continue as a GC.
Significant Delay in Approval of FS
If delay in approval of FS & auditor believes that such delay can be related to events or conditions
relating to GC assessment, perform additional procedures & consider effect on auditor's conclusion.
Financial
Events or Conditions That May Cast Significant Doubt
on Entity's Ability to Continue as a Going Concern (Inter)
Net liability or net current liability position.
. Fixed-term borrowings approaching maturity without realistic prospects of renewal/repayment OR
excessive reliance on short-term borrowings to finance long-term assets.
. Inability to pay creditors on due dates.

Indications of withdrawal of financial support by creditors.

SA 580: Written Representations (WR)
Written statement by mgt to auditor to confirm certain matters or support other audit evidence.
WR in this context don't include FS, assertions therein, or supporting books & records.
WR provide necessary audit evidence, they don't provide SAAE about matters with which they dec
Fact that mgt provided reliable WRs does not affect nature or extent of other audit evidence
auditor obtains about fulfilment of mgt's responsibilities or about specific assertions.
Objectives of Auditor
a) To obtain WR from mgt that they believes they have fulfilled their responsibility for preparation
& completeness of info. provided to auditor
b) To support other audit evidence relevant to FS or specific assertions in FS by means of W
determined necessary by auditor or required by other SAS &
c) To respond appropriately to WR provided by mgt or if mgt don't provide WRs requested by auditor
Form of WR: In form of a representation letter addressed to auditor.
If L/R requires mgt to make written public statements about its responsibilities & auditor deterr
that such statements provide some/all of WRs required, matters covered by such statements nee
be included in representation letter.
Date
Date & Period covered by WR
 WRs are necessary audit evidence, auditor's opinion can't be expressed & report can't be dated be
date of WRs.
-
Further, auditor is concerned with events occurring up to date of audit report that may rec
adjustment or disclosure, WRs are dated as near as practicable to but not after date of audit repo
Sometimes may be appropriate to obtain WR about a specific assertion in FS during audit. In such
it may be necessary to request an updated WR.

01 Standards on Auditing
Period
Learn
Fun
WRs are for all periods referred in audit report because mgt needs to reaffirm that WR it previously
made w.r.t prior periods remain appropriate.
Auditor & mgt may agree to a form of WR that updates WRs relating to prior periods by addressing
whether any changes to such WRs &, if so, what they are.
Situations may arise where current mgt were not present during all periods referred in audit report.
Such persons may assert that they are not in position to provide some or all of WRs because they were
not in place during prior period.
This fact, however, doesn't diminish such persons responsibilities for FS as whole. Accordingly,
requirement for auditor to request WRs that cover all relevant period(s) still applies.
Doubt as to Reliability of Written Representations
If WR are inconsistent with other audit evidence, perform audit procedures to resolve the matter.
If matter remains unresolved, reassess Diligence, Integrity, Competence & Ethical values of mgt &
determine effect on reliability of representations (oral or written) & audit evidence in general.
If auditor concludes WR are not reliable, take appropriate actions, including possible effect on opinion
in audit report as per SA 705 i.e. Disclaimer of Opinion.
Requested Written Representations Not Provided
If mgt doesn't provide one or more of requested WRs, auditor shall:
Discuss matter with mgt
Re-evaluate integrity of mgt & evaluate effect on reliability of representations (oral or written) & audit
evidence in general &
Take appropriate actions, including possible effect on opinion as per SA 705 i.e. Disclaimer of opinion.
Like Auditor gets assurance with WRs, You'll get assurance only after written practice

Principal Auditor (PA)
SA 600: Using the work of Other Auditor
Auditor who uses work performed by other auditors.
Responsibility for reporting on financial info, of entity including financial info, of one or more compo
audited by another auditor.
Other Auditor: Auditor, other than PA responsible for reporting on financial info. of a comp
included in financial info, audited by PA.
"Component" means a division, branch, subsidiary, joint venture, associate or other entity whose find
info. included in financial info. audited by PA.
Note: SA 600 doesn't apply to joint audit nor with auditor's relationship with a predecessor audito
Acceptance as Principal Auditor (July'21) MDRA
Auditor should consider following before accepting his position as PA:
a. Materiality of portion of financial info. which PA audits
b. PA's degree of knowledge regarding business of components
c. RoMM in financial info. of components audited by other auditor
d. Performance of additional procedures regarding components audited by other auditor resulting
having significant participation in such audit.
Materiality of own >> Knowledge of other + RoMM >>> Add. Procedures
Principal Auditor's Procedures (Nov'24)
1. Right to visit component & examine books of a/cs & records, if necessary in special circumstances
2. If planning to use work of other auditor evaluate his competence if he isn't member of ICAI
3. Perform procedures to obtain SAAE that work of other auditor is adequate for PA's purpose.
Advise other auditor of use of his work & co-ordinate at planning stage.
Inform auditor about

☑
Significant Accounting, auditing & reporting requirements &
obtain representation as to compliance with them.
4. PA may discuss or review written summary of his audit procedures in form of questionnaire or che
May also visit other auditor.
5. NTE of audit procedures would depend on his knowledge of professional competence of other
that can be enhanced from review of his previous audit work.

Documentation
A to document in his working papers → components whose financial info, audited by other auditors.
Also document procedures performed & conclusions reached.
Eg. Document discussions with other auditor & review of written summary of other auditor's procedures.
Need not document reasons for limiting procedures where SAAE previously obtained that quality control
P&P followed by other auditor.
Where other auditor's report other than unmodified: Also document how PA dealt with qualifications or
adverse remarks contained in other auditor's report in framing his own report. (Nov'24)
Role of Principal & Other Auditor (Coordination $b/w$ Auditors)
There should be sufficient liaison between principal auditor and other auditor
Role of Principal Auditor:
wad
Necessary to issue written communication(s) as PA to other auditor.
PA should advise other auditor of any matters that come to his attention having important bearing on
other auditor's work.
- When necessary, PA may require other auditor to answer a detailed questionnaire.
Role of Other Auditor:
Other auditor should co-ordinate with PA. For eg, bringing to PA's immediate attention significant
findings to be dealt at entity level, adhering to time-table for audit of component, etc.
Ensure compliance with relevant statutory requirements.
■. Should respond to questionnaire sent by PA on a timely basis.
Reporting Considerations
Qualified or Disclaimer of opinion in case of a limitation on scope of audit
When PA concludes
 work of other auditor can't be used &
✓ unable to perform sufficient additional procedures regarding financial info. of component audited by

Division of Responsibility
➤ PA not responsible for work of other auditors, except in suspicious circumstances about reliab

SA 620: Using work of Auditor's Expert
Auditor's responsibilities regarding use of work in a field of expertise other than accounting or au
to obtain SAAE.
Types of report or opinions Auditor can obtain from Auditor's expert?
 Valuation of complex financial instruments, land & buildings, plant & machinery, jewellery, works
antiques, intangible assets, assets & liabilities in business combinations & impaired assets.
➤ Actuarial calculation of liabilities associated with insurance contracts or employee benefit plans.
➤ Estimation of oil & gas reserves.
➤ Valuation of environmental liabilities, & site clean-up costs.
➤ Interpretation of contracts, laws and regulations.
➤ Analysis of complex or unusual tax compliance issues.
Objectives of Auditor
Determine whether to use work of auditor's expert & if using whether work is adequate.
Areas where Auditor's expert can assist the auditor
Obtaining understanding of entity & its environment, including internal control. (SA 315)
Identifying & assessing ROMM. (SA 315)
Determining & implementing overall responses to assessed risks at FS level. (SA 330)
Ο Designing & performing FAP to respond to assessed risks at assertion level, comprising TO
substantive procedures. (SA 330)
Evaluating sufficiency & appropriateness of audit evidence in forming opinion on FS (SA 700)

✓ Education or professional development in particular field.
This may include formal courses or discussion with individuals possessing expertise.
☑ Discussion with auditors who have performed similar engagements.
When mat has used a mgt's expert
When mgt has used mgt's expert in preparing FS, auditor's decision on whether to use auditor's expert
may also be influenced by such factors. [Same as SA 500]
✓ Nature, scope & objectives of mgt's expert's work.
✓ Whether mgt's expert is employed by entity or engaged by it to provide relevant services.
✓ Extent to which mgt can exercise control or influence work of mgt's expert.
✓ Any controls within entity over mgt's expert's work.
✓ Mgt's expert's competence & capabilities.
✓ Whether mgt's expert is subject to technical performance standards.
Competence, Capability & Objectivity of Auditor's Expert [Similar to SA 500]
Other Relevant Matters include:
• Relevance of auditor's expert's competence to matter, including his areas of specialty.
Eg. Particular actuary may specialise in property & casualty insurance, but has limited expertise regarding
pension calculations.
• Auditor's expert's competence w.rt. relevant accounting & auditing requirements
Eg. Assumptions & methods (including models) are consistent with AFRF.
• Whether unexpected events, changes in conditions or audit evidence indicate need to reconsider initial
evaluation of competence, capabilities & objectivity of auditor's expert.
Evaluating objectivity of auditor's external expert [Similar to SA 500]
When evaluating objectivity of auditor's external expert, may be relevant to:
a) Inquire entity about any known interests or relationships with auditor's external expert.

SA 700: Forming an Opinion & Reporting on FS
Forming opinion on FS
01 Standards on Aud
   Obtain reasonable assurance whether FS are free from material misstatement, due to fraud or er
 Form opinion, whether FS are prepared in all material respects as per AFRF.
Further, when FS prepared as per fair presentation framework, evaluate whether FS achien
presentation considering:
1. Overall presentation, structure & content of FS &
2. Whether FS, including notes achieve fair presentation.
In other words, express unmodified opinion when auditor concludes FS are prepared, in all m
respects, as per AFRF.
1. Title: Independent Auditor's Report
Basic Elements of Audit Report
2. Addressee: As per circumstances of engg.
Maybe addressed to Members for general purpose (statutory) FS & BOD for special purpose FS
3. Auditor's Opinion: First section of auditor's report
Opinion section of auditor's report shall also:
a) Identify entity whose FS have been audited
b) State that FS have been audited.
c) Identify title of each statement comprising FS.
d) Refer to notes, including summary of significant $a/c$ policies
e) Specify date or period covered by, each FS comprising the FS.
When expressing unmodified opinion on FS prepared as per fair presentation framework, use cra
following phrases, regarded as equal:
(i) In our opinion, FS present fairly, in all material respects, [...] as per [AFRF] or
(ii) In our opinion, FS give a true and fair view of [...] as per [AFRF].
When expressing unmodified opinion on FS prepared as per compliance framework, auditor's opinions
be that accompanying FS are prepared, in all material respects, as per [the AFRF].
If reference to AFRF in opinion is not to $A/c$ Standards, identify origin of such other framework
4. Basis for Opinion section states that:
a. States that audit was conducted as per Standards on Auditing
. Refers to Section of audit report that describes auditor's responsibilities under SAs
 . Includes Statement that auditor is independent of entity & has fulfilled other ethical responsib
Refer to Code of Ethics issued by ICAI
States whether auditor obtained SAAE to provide basis for auditor's opinion.
Going Concern: Already covered in SA 570.
Note: Where GC Basis is appropriate, no special para required in Audit Report for this fact.

(ISA)
dby
Objective of Auditor
To enhance communicative value of audit report by providing greater transparency about audit.
To assist user in understanding matters that, in auditor's professional judgment, were of most
significance in audit of FS of current period.
Communicating KAM is not:
A substitute for
a) disclosures in FS as per AFRF
b) auditor expressing a modified opinion as per SA 705
c) reporting as per SA 570 when a material uncertainty exists relating to events or conditions that
may cast significant doubt on an entity's ability to continue as a GC
d) Separate opinion on individual matters.

Factors → Determining Key Audit Matters
01 Standards on Auditi
Auditor shall determine, from matters communicated with TCWG, those matters that $reqj=$
significant auditor attention. In making this determination, auditor shall take into account the follow
a. Areas of higher assessed RoMM or significant risks identified as per SA 315
b. Significant auditor judgments relating to areas in FS that involved significant mgt judgment, inclu
a/c estimates having high estimation uncertainty.
c. Effect on audit of significant events or transactions that occurred during the period.
ASE
SEA
✓
Eg. of Key Audit Matters
Impairment Assessment, Provision for losses & contingencies, Valuation of financial instruments, Reve
recognition, Taxation matters (multiple tax jurisdictions, uncertain tax position, deferred tax assets
Communicating Key Audit Matters
Introductory language in this section of audit report shall state that:
a) KAM are those matters that, in auditor's professional judgment, which were of most significance in c
of FS [of current period] &
b) These matters were addressed in context of audit of FS & forming auditor's opinion & auditor doe
provide separate opinion on these matters.
Can matters other than disclosed in FS be KAM?
There may be other matters communicated with TCWG that required significant auditor attentio
therefore may be determined to be KAM.
Such matters may include matters relevant to audit not required to be disclosed in FS.
For eg, implementation of new IT system (or significant changes to existing IT system) during per
may be area of significant auditor attention, if such change had significant effect on overall a
strategy or related to significant risk (e.g. changes to a system affecting revenue recognition).
Placement of KAM section in auditor's report?
 After Basis for Opinion paragraph & before Mgt's Responsibility para.
➤ In case, MURG section is required as per SA 570, then KAM is placed after that.
As per SA 706: EOM para may be either directly before or after KAM section, as per auditor's judgm
What to do if no KAM to communicate?

1.70
Factors Determining Key Audit Matters
Auditor shall determine, from matters communicated with TCWG, those matters that
significant auditor attention. In making this determination, auditor shall take into account the
a. Areas of higher assessed RoMM or significant risks identified as per SA 315
b. Significant auditor judgments relating to areas in FS that involved significant mgt judgment
$a/c$ estimates having high estimation uncertainty.
c. Effect on audit of significant events or transactions that occurred during the period.
A
Eg. of Key Audit Matters
Impairment Assessment, Provision for losses & contingencies, Valuation of financial instruments
recognition, Taxation matters (multiple tax jurisdictions, uncertain tax position, deferred tax
Communicating Key Audit Matters
Introductory language in this section of audit report shall state that:
 ) KAM are those matters that, in auditor's professional judgment, which were of most significanc
of FS [of current period] &
) These matters were addressed in context of audit of FS & forming auditor's opinion & audit
provide separate opinion on these matters.
Can matters other than disclosed in FS be KAM?
There may be other matters communicated with TCWG that required significant auditor at
therefore may be determined to be KAM.

may be area of significant auditor attention, if such change had significant effect on ove
strategy or related to significant risk (e.g. changes to a system affecting revenue recognition

 Standards on
SA 705, "Modifications to the Opinion in the Independent Auditor's Report"
Objective: To express modified opinion on FS when:
Auditor concludes, based on audit evidence obtained. FS as a whole are not free from MM or
Auditor is unable to obtain SAAE to conclude that FS as a whole are free from MM
Definition of Pervasive: Describe effects/possible effects (SAAE) of Misstatements on FS
Pervasive effects on FS are those that, in auditor's judgement:
Not confined to specific elements, $a/cs$ or items of FS
$\}z$
 If confined, represent a substantial proportion of FS1
In relation to disclosures, fundamental to users' understanding of FS
Qualified(Q) Opinion: Express qualified opinion when:
Fun
1
a. Obtained SAAE concludes that misstatements, individually or in aggregate, are material, but not
pervasive to FS OR
b. Unable to obtain SAAE to base the opinion, but concludes that possible effects on FS of undetected
misstatements, could be material but not pervasive. (Material but not pervasive)
Spl. Considerations: When auditor expresses (Q) opinion due to MM in F.S, auditor shall state that, in
auditor's opinion, except for effects of matter(s) described in Basis for (Q) Opinion section:
1. When reporting as per fair presentation framework, accompanying F.S. present fairly, in all material
respects (or give a true and fair view of) [...] in accordance with [applicable FRF] or
2. When reporting as per compliance framework, accompanying F.S. have been prepared, in all material
respects, in accordance with [applicable FRF].
When modification arises from inability to obtain SAAE, auditor shall use corresponding phrase "except
for possible effects of matter(s) ..." for modified opinion.
Adverse(A) Opinion: Obtained SAAE, concludes misstatements, individually or in the aggregate, are both
material & pervasive to FS.
Spl. Considerations: When auditor expresses an (A) opinion, auditor shall state that, in auditor's opinion,
because of significance of matter(s) described in Basis for (A) Opinion section:
When reporting as per a fair presentation framework, accompanying F.S. do not present fairly (or give c
2 true and fair view of) [...] in accordance with [applicable FRF] or
When reporting as per compliance framework, accompanying F.S. have not been prepared, in all materia
respects, in accordance with [applicable FRF]. Fls don.
Disclaimer of Opinion: Unable to obtain SAAE & concludes that possible effects on FS of undetecte
misstatements, could be both material & pervasive.

01 Standards on Auditi
Note: Unless required by law or regulation, when auditor disclaims opinion on FS, auditor's reports
not include KAM section as per SA 701.
Spl considerations: When auditor disclaims opinion due to inability to obtain SAAE, auditor shall:
1. State that auditor doesn't express opinion on FS.
2. State that, because of significance of matter(s) described in Basis for Disclaimer of Opinion sectio
auditor not been able to obtain SAAE to provide basis for audit opinion on FS &
3. Amend statement required in SA 700, which indicates that FS have been audited, to state that audi
was engaged to audit the FS.
Note: If Auditor expresses Adverse or Disclaimer of Opinion then audit report shall not conte
unmodified opinion w.r.t any element of F.S.
Basis for Modification of Opinion (Qualified/Disclaimer /Adverse)?
When auditor modifies (Q/ D/ A) opinion, auditor shall:
a. Amend the heading “Basis for Opinion" to "Basis for Qualified/Adverse/Disclaimer of Opinion" &
b. Within this section, include description of matter giving rise to modification.
Section
When auditor disclaims opinion on FS, A/R shall not include following elements required by SA 700:
a. Reference to section of A/R where auditor's responsibilities are described &
b. Statement about whether audit evidence obtained is sufficient & appropriate to provide basis
auditor's opinion.
Note: Even if auditor expressed A/D opinion, describe in Basis for Opinion section reasons for any of
matters of which auditor is aware that would have required a modification to opinion & effects ther
a.

SEBI Provisions on Auditor Resignation (Listed entities)
If auditor propose to resign
within 45 days from end of qtr →
before resignation issue Limited
Review (LR) Report for such qtr

ICAI announced that auditor of unlisted Co. shall not mention "professional pre-occupation" as reason
for resignation. Auditor shall clearly mention reasons for resignation in resignation letter issued to Co.

When auditor expects to modify opinion, auditor shall communicate with TCWG:
circumstances that led to expected modification & wording of modification.

T
) SA 570, if MU exists & adequate disclosure not there: No disclosure: Adverse Opinion &
Incomplete disclosure: Qualified Opinion

ii) Unable to obtain evidence about JV investment (representing 90% of entity's net assets) → Disclaime
v) No agreement of a Loan or Advance (material) → Qualified Opinion

a) Auditor not required to modify opinion as per SA 705 as a result of the matter & αρτυρη
b) When SA 701 applies, matter not determined to be KAM.
EOM
When to include KAM?
(zamatta)
SAs contain specific requirements for auditor to include EOM para in audit report.
These circumstances include:
When a FRF prescribed by law or regulation would be unacceptable but for the fact that it is prescribed
by law or regulation
To alert users that FS are prepared as per special purpose framework
When facts become known to auditor after date of audit report & auditor provides a new or amended
audit report (i.e., subsequent events)
Examples of circumstances where auditor may consider it necessary to include an Emphasis of Matter
paragraph are:
 An uncertainty relating to future outcome of exceptional litigation or regulatory action.
- A significant subsequent event that occurs between date of F.S and date of auditor's report.
Applied
Early application (where permitted) of new a/c std that has material effect on F.S.
A major catastrophe that has had, or continues to have, significant effect on entity's financial position
How to include EOM Para?
When auditor includes EOM para in audit report, auditor shall:
Include in a separate section of audit report with heading that includes the term "Emphasis of Matter'
Include in the para clear reference to matter being emphasized & where relevant disclosures that fully
describe the matter can be found in FS. Para shall refer only to info. presented or disclosed in FS &
Indicate that auditor's opinion is not modified in respect of matter emphasized.
EOM para, isn't a substitute for: Modified opinion as per SA 705/ Disclosures in FS/Reporting as per
5A 570 (Revised) when a material uncertainty.... [माँ मुझे याद आ रहा है]

Learn
With
Fun
In audit, statutory auditor noticed some matters. He wants to draw user's attention towards such
matters, though his opinion is not modified for such matters.
Draft relevant paras of audit report for following matters:
i) Co. has plan to resume its construction activities w.r.t one of its thermal power project, activity of
such power plant was suspended in FY 2023-24. Thermal power project comprises of plant &
equipment amounting to 5.95 Cr & capital work in progress of 147.50 Cr.
ii) FS of 5 branches are included in Standalone FS whose FS reflect tatal assets of 90 crores as at
31.03.2024 & total revenue from operations of 40 crores. FS of these branches have been audited
by branch auditors.
Solution
Emphasis of Matter
We draw attention to following note of standalone FS:
Note 27 regarding plans of Company to resume construction/developmental activities of a thermal power
project. Carrying amounts related to project as at 31st March, 2024 comprise of plant & equipment of
5.95 crore & capital work in progress of 147.50 crore. Our opinion is not modified in respect of this
matter.
Other Matter
We did not audit FS of 5 branches included in Standalone FS of Co. whose FS reflect total assets of
90 Cr as at 31.03.2024 & total revenue from operations of 40 Cr for year ended on that date.
FS of these branches have been audited by branch auditors & our opinion in so far as it relates to the
amounts & disclosures of these branches, is based solely on report of branch auditors. Our opinion is not
modified in respect of this matter.
