SA 710: Comparative Information - Corresponding Figures & Comparative FS
Comparative Info: Amounts and disclosures included in FS for one or more prior periods as per AFRE
Essential audit reporting differences between approaches are:
a. For corresponding figures, auditor's opinion on FS refers to current period only whereas
b. For comparative FS, auditor's opinion refers to each period for which FS are presented.
Audit Procedures for Comparative Information:
Auditor shall evaluate whether:
a) Comparative info. agrees with amounts & other disclosures in prior period &
b)
$A/c$ policies are consistent with current period or if there have been changes, whether changes he
been properly accounted, presented & disclosed.
Material Misstatement:
➤ If auditor becomes aware of possible material misstatement in comparative info, perform additio
audit procedures to obtain SAAE to determine whether material misstatement exists.
➤ If auditor audited prior period's FS, also follow requirements of SA 560.
☐
Written Representations:
☑ As per SA 580, auditor shall request WR for all periods referred to in auditor's opinion.
☑
Also obtain specific WR regarding any prior period item separately disclosed in current year's P&L.
Basic Μ.Μ. 560 & 580
Audit reporting for Corresponding Figures
Auditor's opinion shall not refer to corresponding figures except in following circumstances:
1. If audit report on prior period included modified opinion & matter which gave rise to modification
Sunresolved, modify audit opinion on current period's FS.
In Basis for Modification para in A/R, auditor shall either:
a) Refer to both current period's fig. & corresponding fig. in description of matter giving rise
modification when effects or possible effects of matter on current period's figures are material Ok
b) In other cases, explain that audit opinion modified because of effects or possible effects of unresol
matter on comparability of current period & corresponding fig.
2. If auditor obtains audit evidence that MM exists in prior period FS on which unmodified opin
previously issued, verify whether misstatement has been resolved &, if not, express qualified or adve
opinion in audit report on current period FS.

01 Standards on Auditing
Reporting for Comparative F.S.
Auditor's opinion shall refer to each period for which FS are presented.
POAIL
Learn
Fun
When reporting on current period's audit, if auditor's opinion on such prior period FS differs from opinion
previously issued on such FS, disclose substantive reason for different opinion in OM para.
If auditor concludes that material misstatement is present in previously audited figures of F.S, he should
report it to mgt and request that predecessor auditor be informed.
If then prior years statements are amended with new report by predecessor auditor, then auditor shall
report only on current period.
Reporting treatment common to both (for corresponding figures and comparative information)
If FS of prior period audited by predecessor auditor, auditor shall state in OM Para:
That FS of prior period were audited by a predecessor auditor
Type of opinion expressed by predecessor auditor
Date of that audit report.
If prior period FS not audited than report in OM Para that corresponding/comparative figures are
unaudited. However, disclosure doesn't relieve him from his responsibility of obtaining SAAE that
opening balances do not contain misstatements that materially affect current period's FS.
SA 720: Auditor's Responsibility in Relation to Other Information
Scope
Deals with auditor's responsibilities relating to other info. (financial or non-financial) contained in Annual
Report.
Auditor's opinion on FS doesn't cover other info. (Annual Report)
He's just required to read & consider other info. for any materially inconsistent info. from FS that may
indicate MM in either FS or Annual Report.
Auditor's responsibility apply whether other info. is received prior to or after auditor's report
Note: This SA doesn't apply to preliminary announcements of financial info. or prospectus.
Obtaining the Other Information
Determine, through discussion with mgt, which document(s) comprises annual report & entity's planne
manner & timing of issuance of such document(s)
Make arrangements with mgt to obtain in timely manner &, if possible, prior to date of audit report, fin
version of annual report &
When annual report not available until the date of audit report, request mgt to provide a WR that fir
version of document(s) will be provided to auditor when available & prior to its issuance by entity, su
that auditor can complete procedures required by this SA.
1.77

tor's opinion shall refer to each period for which FS are presented.

Tously issued on such FS, disclose substantive reason for different opinion in OM para.

ort.

01 Standards on
Reading & Considering the Other Information
Auditor shall read the other info & shall:
Consider whether there is material inconsistency between
• other info & FS &
other info. & auditor's knowledge obtained in audit.
While reading other info, remain alert for indications that other info may be materially misst
Responding when Material Inconsistency Appears to Exist or
Other Info. Appears to be Materially Misstated
Discuss the matter with mgt &, if necessary, perform other procedures to conclude whether:
a. A material misstatement of other info. exists
b. A material misstatement of FS exists or
SA-705
c. Auditor's understanding of entity & its environment needs to be updated. SA-315
When other info. is materially misstated
Request mgt to correct other info → if mgt:
a) Agrees to make correction, determine that correction made OR
b) Refuses to make correction, communicate with TCWG & request that correction be made.
If auditor concludes MM exists in other info. obtained prior to date of auditor's report, & o
isn't corrected after communicating with TCWG, take appropriate action, including:
a) Considering implications for auditor's report & communicating with TCWG about how audito
address the MM in auditor's report OR
Hao
b) Withdrawing from engg, where withdrawal possible under applicable $L/R$.
If auditor concludes that MM exists in other info. obtained after date of audit report:
a. If other info. is corrected, perform necessary procedures OR
b. If other info. isn't corrected after communicating with TCWG, take appropriate action co
auditor's legal rights & obligations, to bring uncorrected MM to attention of users.
When FS are materially misstated or Auditor's understanding needs to be updated
Respond as per other SAs.
Reporting
Audit Report shall include separate section with a heading "Other Info." when, at date of aud
a. For audit of FS of listed entity, auditor obtained or expects to obtain, other info or
b. For audit of FS of unlisted corporate entity, auditor obtained some or all of other info.
a.

CHAPTER
02
a) Integrity:
Professional Ethics
Fundamental Principles
Be straightforward & honest in professional & business relationships.
Not knowingly be associated where info.
(a) Contains a materially false or misleading statement
(b) Contains statements or info. provided negligently or
(c) Omits required info. that would be misleading.
Lear
Fun
b) Objectivity: Do not to compromise professional or business judgment because of bias, conflict of
interest or undue influence of others
c) Professional Competence & Due Care (PCDC):
Attain & maintain professional knowledge & skill
Act diligently as per professional standards
Serving clients & employers with professional competence requires sound judgment in applyin
professional knowledge & skill.
Requires continuing awareness & understanding of technical, professional & business developments.
Continuing professional development to develop & maintain capabilities to perform competently.
Diligence encompasses responsibility to act as per requirements of assignment on a timely basis.
Take reasonable steps to ensure those working under accountant's authority have training & supervisio
d) Confidentiality: Do not disclose info. acquired from client or employer (including prospective). Not us
such info for personal advantage. Continues even after relationship has ended.
Circumstances where professional accountants might be required to disclose confidential info:
• Disclosure is required by law Eg. Legal proceedings
Disclosure is permitted by law & authorized by client or employing org
There is a professional duty or right to disclose, when not prohibited by law:
i. To comply with Peer Review or Quality Review of ICAI
4
ii. To respond to inquiry or investigation by professional or regulatory body
iii. To protect professional interests in legal proceedings
iv. To comply with technical & professional standards, including ethics requirements.
In deciding whether to disclose confidential info. professional accountants should consider followin
a) Whether interests of any party, including 3rd parties might be affected
b) Whether all relevant info is known & substantiated, &
c) Proposed type of communication & to whom it is addressed
14
d) Whether parties to whom communication is addressed are appropriate recipients.
e) Professional Behaviour: Comply with laws & regulations & avoid conduct that might discredit
profession.

Exaggerated claims for services or qualifications or experience or
b. Disparaging references or comparisons to work of others.

Types of Threats
Confidentiality
Self-interest threat: financial or other interest that influence judgment or behaviour
Self-review threat: Not appropriately evaluate results of previous judgment or activity performed
Advocacy threat: Promote client's/employer's position to the point that objectivity is compromised
 Familiarity threat: Long or close relationship with client/employer professional accountant will be
too sympathetic to their interests
 Intimidation threat: Deterred from acting objectively because of pressures, including undue influence.

Safeguards to Address Threats
AADAS
Examples of Actions that might be safeguards to address threats include:

 Having appropriate reviewer, not team member, review work to address self-review threat.
 Using different partners & engg. teams with separate reporting lines for non-assurance service
- Involving another firm to perform part of engg.
 Separating teams when dealing with matters of confidential nature.

Matters out of Purview: Clearly insignificant or personal misconduct unrelated to client business.

Applicability of NOCLAR

CAIP: Audit engg, of entities listed on Recognised Stock Exchange in India with net worth >= 250 cr &
Members in service: Senior Professional Accountants (directors, officers or senior employees) in
service, being employees of listed entities.
Documentation Requirements in NOCLAR:
How mgt/ TCWG responded to the matter.
Course of action accountant considered, judgments & decisions taken..
How accountant is satisfied that responsibility of public interest has been fulfilled.
NOCLAR vs. SA 250
wole
SA 250 is applicable only on Audit & not other Assurance engagements. However, NOCLAR is applicable
on professional accountants in service & practice.
- SA 250 talks of auditor's responsibilities for laws having direct effect on determination of material
amounts & disclosures in FS (eq. tax & labour laws) & other laws & regulations that don't have direct
effect on FS, but compliance may be fundamental to business. NOCLAR also considers non-compliance
that causes substantial harm resulting in serious consequences (financial/non-financial).
SA 250 doesn't define stakeholders. NOCLAR is related to investors, creditors, employees & public.

Penalty for Falsely Claiming to be a Member
02 Professional Ethics
Sec 24 provides that any person who-
not being a member of ICAI represents that he's member of ICAI or uses designation CA
practice as a CA,
 ) being a member of ICAI, but not having COP, represents he's in practice or
punishable on 1st conviction with fine upto 1000 & on subsequent conviction with imprisonment upto
6 months or fine upto 5,000, or both.,
Significance of Certificate of Practice
As per Section 6 of CA Act, 1949:-
7 No member of ICAI shall practice in India or elsewhere unless obtained from Council a COP..
 ) Every such member shall pay annual fee.
 ) COP may be cancelled by Council under prescribed circumstances.
Note: Member not in practice can't accept engagement to render services of any type normally
prescribed for a CA, even though he does not require special qualifications for doing so..
A CAIP suspended from practice for 6 months & surrendered his COP. During period of suspension,
member did not undertake any audit assignments, he undertook representation assignments for income
tax whereby he would appear before tax authorities in his capacity as a CA.
CA not holding COP can't take any other work because it would be violation of CA Act, 1949.
 If member is suspended & not holding COP, he cannot in any other capacity take up any practice
separable from his capacity to practice as a member of ICAI.
• This is because once a person becomes a CA, he's bound by CA Act, 1949 & its Regulations.
violation
• He cannot set Regulations at naught by contending, he's entitled to practice in some other capacity.
• If he appears before tax authorities, he is doing so only in his capacity as CA & member of ICAI.
Conclusion: CA would not be allowed to represent before income tax authorities for period he remains
suspended. Accordingly, in he is guilty of professional misconduct.
If
What if, Suspended CAiP designating himself as Data Privacy consultant, filing Data Privacy related
returns & appearance as consultant before related authorities in capacity other than CAIP → Allowed
Cancellation & Restoration of Certificate of Practice (COP)
COP shall be cancelled, if:
name is removed from the Register
member has ceased to practise
Council satisfied that COP issued on basis of incorrect, misleading or false info or by mistake
member has not paid annual fee for COP till 30th day of September of relevant year.
Where COP is cancelled, holder shall surrender it to the Secretary.

Penalty for Falsely Claiming to be a Member
Sec 24 provides that any person who-
02 Professional Ethics
not being a member of ICAI → represents that he's member of ICAI or uses designation CA
being a member of ICAI, but not having COP, represents he's in practice or practice as a CA,
punishable on 1st conviction with fine upto 1000 & on subsequent conviction with imprisonment upto
6 months or fine upto 5,000, or both..
Significance of Certificate of Practice
As per Section 6 of CA Act, 1949:-
No member of ICAI shall practice in India or elsewhere unless obtained from Council a COP..
Every such member
shall p
Il pay annual fee.
COP may be cancelled by Council under prescribed circumstances.
Note: Member not in practice can't accept engagement to render services of any type normally
prescribed for a CA, even though he does not require special qualifications for doing so.
A CAIP suspended from practice for 6 months & surrendered his COP. During period of suspension,
member did not undertake any audit assignments, he undertook representation assignments for income
tax whereby he would appear before tax authorities in his capacity as a CA.
CA not holding COP can't take any other work because it would be violation of CA Act, 1949.
If member is suspended & not holding COP, he cannot in any other capacity take up any practice
separable from his capacity to practice as a member of ICAI.
• This is because once a person becomes a CA, he's bound by CA Act, 1949 & its Regulations.
He cannot set Regulations at naught by contending, he's entitled to practice in some other capacity.
If he appears before tax authorities, he is doing so only in his capacity as CA & member of ICAI.
Liviol
Conclusion: CA would not be allowed to represent before income tax authorities for period he remains
suspended. Accordingly, in he is quilty of professional misconduct.
Im
What if, Suspended CAiP designating himself as Data Privacy consultant, filing Data Privacy related
returns & appearance as consultant before related authorities in capacity other than CAIP → Allowed

COP shall be cancelled, if:
name is removed from the Register
member has ceased to practise
Council satisfied that COP issued on basis of incorrect, misleading or false info or by mistake
member has not paid annual fee for COP till 30th day of September of relevant year.
Where COP is cancelled, holder shall surrender it to the Secretary.

Members deemed to be in Practice
As per Sec 2(2): Member of ICAI shall be deemed "to be in practice" if he:
i. engages in practice of accountancy or
ii. offers to perform or performs service involving auditing or preparation, verification or certifica-
of FS or holds himself out as accountant or
iii. renders professional services about matters of principle or accounting, presentation
certification of financial facts/data or

Advisor or consultant to an issue, including such matters as:
02 Professional Ethics ics
 Drafting prospectus. Drafting & filing of listing agreement & formalities with Stock Exchanges,
Registrar of Companies & SEBI.
 Preparation of publicity budget, advice regarding selection of:
(i) ad-media, (ii) centres for holding conferences, (iii) bankers to issue,
(iv) collection centres, (v) brokers (vi) underwriters.
✓ Advice for selecting agencies connected with issue, like Registrars to Issue, printers & advertising.
✓ Advice on post issue activities
e.g., follow up steps which include listing of instruments & dispatch of certificates & refunds.
Explanation - Portfolio mgt, underwriting & broking (PUB) not permitted.
✓ Investment counselling for securities
✓ Registrar to an issue & for transfer of shares/other securities.
✓ Quality, Environment, Energy Audit.
✓ Recovery Consultant in Banking Sector.NPAls
Insurance Financial Advisory Services under IRDA Act 1999, including Insurance Brokerage.
✓ Insolvency Professional in terms of Insolvency & Bankruptcy Code, 2016
✓ Admin Services: Such services require no professional judgment & clerical in nature.
Examples of administrative services include:
Word processing services.
• Preparing administrative or statutory forms for client.
• Submitting such forms as instructed by the client.
• Monitoring statutory filing dates, & advising an audit client of those dates.
Note: CA is deemed to be in practice during period he renders 'service with armed forces'
Companies not to practice as CAs If LLP has Co. as partner it can't engage in practice
Member in Practice Prohibited from using Designation Other than Chartered Accountant - Sec 7
• Merchant Banker / Advisor to an issue: Members may obtain registration as category IV Merchant
Banker & act as Advisor or Consultant to issue. In client Companies' offer documents & ads regarding
capital issue, name & address of CA/ firm can be indicated under the caption "Advisor/Consultant
to Issue". However, name & address of such CA/firm should not appear prominently..
• Members of ICAI who are also Directors in Companies, members of Political parties or CAs Cells in
political parties, holding different positions in clubs are not permitted to mention these positions.
 Member can't designate as Cost Accountant; can use A.C.M.A (Associate) or F.C.M.A (Fellow).
 Permitted to mention membership of foreign Institute of Accountancy, recognized by Council
through MOU / Mutual Recognition Agreement (MRA) with ICAI.
 Improper for CA to state on professional docs Income-tax Consultant, Cost Accountant,
Company Secretary, Cost Consultant or a Management Consultant.
 Designation "Corporate Lawyer" not permitted.

 CAiP who are otherwise eligible may also practice as Company Secretaries &/or Cost Accountants.
Not use designation/s of those Institute/s simultaneously with "Chartered Accountant".
• Use of initials 'CPA' not permitted on visiting cards.
Permission to mention qualifications of certain Institutions: Members permitted to mention a title on
their visiting cards to indicate membership of a foreign Institute of Accountancy, which is recognised
 by Council e.g. South African Institute of Chartered Accountants (SAICA), Institute of Certified
Public Accountants (CPA Ireland) & Institute of Chartered Accountants in England & Wales (ICAEW)
Branch office (Section 27)
GA
If CAIP or Firm has >1 office in India, each office should be in separate charge of member of ICAI
To comply with requirement of being in charge of office:
Member to reside in place office situated/attends the office >= 182 days
Council Decisions:
i) Use of name-board: Nameboard in place of residence allowed with designation of CA, provided it is
nameplate of individual member & not of firm.
CA
ii) Exemptiongranted to member or CA firm to have a 2nd office without being under separate charge
of member of ICAI, provided-
a. 2nd office is in same premises, in which 1st office is located or
b. 2nd office is in same city, in which 1st office is located or
c. 2nd office is located within 50 km. from municipal limits of a city, in which 1st office is located
Note: Member having 2 offices of type referred above shall declare, which is his main office, which
would constitute his professional address.
1. Mr. K, CAIP has office in suburbs of Chennai. He opens another office which is within the city & at
distance of 30 km. from his office in suburb. For new office, he has employed a retired Income To
Commissioner who is not a CA. No misconduct. However, declare which is the main office.
2. Mr. X & Mr. Y, partners of CA Firm, one in-charge of HO & another of Branch at a distance of 80 k
from municipal limits, puts up a name-board of firm in both premises & also their respective residenc
CAs are guilty of misconduct. Distance given in the question is not relevant for deciding.
Exemption for Hilly Areas
Temporary office in plains allowed for limited period not exceeding 3 months pa,
No need to close regular office & correspondence made at regular office
Name board on femp. office not displayed when its not functional
Temp. office not mentioned on Visiting cards/letter heads as Place Of Business
Before winters, member/firm informs ICAI about opening temp office from particular date & af
closure intimate ICAI via regd post.

02 Professional Ethics
First Schedule
PART I - Professional Misconduct in relation to CAs in Practice (CAIP)
A CAIP is deemed to be guilty of professional misconduct if he:
Clause (1):
allows any person to practice in his name as a CA
Lewa
W
Fur
unless such person is also a CAIP & is in partnership with or employed by him. like Ashok fram
Above clause is intended to safeguard public against unqualified accountant practicing under the cov
of qualified accountants.
✓ It ensures that work of accountant carried out by a CA who may be his partner or employee & wo
under his control & supervision.
Who can practice in name of CAIP?
CAIP Pagues/Employee
Mr. C, CAIP allowed his brother-in-law Mr. P who is not a CA, to practice in name of CA. C. Also allow
Mr. T who is employee in his firm to practice in the name. Whether Mr. C is correct in allowing
brother-in-law Mr. P & Mr. T employee of his firm to practice in his name.
Clause (1) reference. In given situation Mr. CAiP allowed a non-CA his brother-in-law Mr. P to practic
name of CA. C is not correct. However, he can allow Mr. T who is employee in his firm to practice in
name.
Conclusion: Thus, CA. C will be held guilty of professional misconduct for allowing Mr. P who is not a
to practice in his name as a CA as per Clause (1) of Part I to the First Schedule.
Clause (2):
[Assumed that Mr T is a CaiPlas Q. clearly mentions Mr. P isn't a CA
pays or allows or agrees to pay or allow, directly or indirectly,
• any share, commission or brokerage in the fees or profits of his professional business,
to any person
• other than member of ICAI or
• partner or retired partner or legal representative of deceased partner or
• member of any other professional body
or persons having such qualification as may be brescr

02 Professional Ethics
CS
Sharing audit fees with State Govt: Registrars of various State Coop. Societies require certain amount.
fee is payable to concerned SG & auditor has to deposit a percentage of audit fee in state
of audit fee
Treasury by prescribed challan within prescribed time of receipt of Audit fee.
Council allowed to accept such assignment wherein a percentage of professional fee is deducted by
Govt to meet administrative & other expenditure.
Example: CAIP gave 50% of audit fees to complainant, not a CA, under nomenclature of office
allowance & this continued for no. of years, held by Council that in substance CA shared his profits
& was guilty of professional misconduct. It isn't nomenclature that is material, but substance of
transaction.
Note: Paying % of profits to article as stipend not allowed even if financial condition weak.
Share of Profit/Sale of Goodwill (Death Cases)
Partnership Firm: Legal representative (LR) will continue to receive share if Deed provides for it.
Sole Proprietorship (SP): Maluck
CA Rafeeu
No sharing of fees between LR & purchaser of Goodwill on death of SP +
allowed if agreement allows.
Goodwill can be transferred to other CA if:
☑
payments in instalments
Sale completed within 1 year of death (permission of council required)
✓ If dispute of legal heir → inform ICAI within 1 year about dispute & name preserved for 1 year
from dispute settlement.
CA Ravi, a CAIP, was proprietor of M/s Ravi & Associates. CA Ravi died on 15.09.24 due to cardiac
arrest. Only family member left behind was his wife, Roohi. On 30.09.25, Roohi sold practice of her
husband to CA Balwan for 25 Lacs with right to use firm name i.e., $M/s$. Ravi & Associates & requested
ICAI to consider effect of such sale.
Give your comments on following issues w.r.t CA Act, 1949 and Schedules thereto:
(i) Whether Roohi can sell practice to CA Balwan?
(ii) Can CA Balwan continue to practice as proprietor in name of $M/s$ Ravi & Associates?
Sale of Goodwill:
W.rt. Clause (2) of Part I to First Schedule to CA Act, 1949, Council considered whether goodwill of
proprietary concern of CA can be sold to another eligible member after death of proprietor.
• Legal heir of deceased member has to obtain Council permission within a year of death of proprietor
Sale is completed/effected in all respects & ICAI's permission is sought within a year of death c
proprietor.
• For these cases, name of proprietary firm would be kept in abeyance (i.e. not removed on receipt
info. about death of proprietor) only upto a period of one year from death of proprietor.
Facts
In given case, Mrs. Roohi, widow of Mr. Ravi sold the practice along with right to use firm name af
one year of his death for 25 lakhs. This sale is in effect sale of goodwill.
2.12
wah
Fun
02 Professional Ethics
Sharing audit fees with State Govt: Registrars of various State Coop. Societies require certain amount
of audit fee is payable to concerned SG & auditor has to deposit a percentage of audit fee in state
Treasury by prescribed challan within prescribed time of receipt of Audit fee.
Council allowed to accept such assignment wherein a percentage of professional fee is deducted by
Govt to meet administrative & other expenditure.
Example: CAIP gave 50% of audit fees to complainant, not a CA, under nomenclature of office
allowance & this continued for no. of years, held by Council that in substance CA shared his profits
& was guilty of professional misconduct. It isn't nomenclature that is material, but substance of
transaction.
Note: Paying % of profits to article as stipend not allowed even if financial condition weak
Share of Profit/Sale of Goodwill (Death Cases)
Partnership Firm: Legal representative (LR) will continue to receive share if Deed provides for it.
Sole Proprietorship (SP):
 
CA Rajeev
No sharing of fees between LR & purchaser of Goodwill on death of SP +
allowed if agreement allows.
Goodwill can be transferred to other CA if:
payments in instalments
✓ Sale completed within 1 year of death (permission of council required)
☑ If dispute of legal heir → inform ICAI within 1 year about dispute & name preserved for 1 year
from dispute settlement.
CA Ravi, a CAiP, was proprietor of M/s Ravi & Associates. CA Ravi died on 15.09.24 due to cardiac
rrest. Only family member left behind was his wife, Roohi. On 30.09.25, Roohi sold practice of her
usband to CA Balwan for  25 Lacs with right to use firm name i.e., M/s. Ravi & Associates & requested
CAI to consider effect of such sale.
ive your comments on following issues w.r.t CA Act, 1949 and Schedules thereto:
) Whether Roohi can sell practice to CA Balwan?
i) Can CA Balwan continue to practice as proprietor in name of M/s Ravi & Associates?
ale of Goodwill:
.rt. Clause (2) of Part I to First Schedule to CA Act, 1949, Council considered whether goodwill o
Coprietary concern of CA can be sold to another eligible member after death of proprietor.
Legal heir of deceased member has to obtain Council permission within a year of death of proprieto
Sale is completed/effected in all respects & ICAI's permission is sought within a year of death
proprietor.
For these cases, name of proprietary firm would be kept in abeyance (i.e. not removed on receipt
info. about death of proprietor) only upto a period of one year from death of proprietor.
cts
given case, Mrs. Roohi, widow of Mr. Ravi sold the practice along with right to use firm name aft
e year of his death for₹25 lakhs This sale is in effect sale of goodwill
02 Professional Ethics
From the discussion given above it can be concluded that:
ⅰ) Mrs. Roohi cannot sell the practice of CA. Balwan with right to use firm name.
ii) CA Balwan can't continue to practice in name of firm $M/s$. Ravi & Associates because nar
be kept in abeyance only up to a period of 1 year from death of proprietor.

02 Professional Ethics

Lewu

Fun
i) Mrs. Roohi cannot sell the practice of CA. Balwan with right to use firm name.
iⅱ) CA Balwan can't continue to practice in name of firm M/s. Ravi & Associates because name would
be kept in abeyance only up to a period of 1 year from death of proprietor.
Clause (3): accepts or agrees to accept any part of profits of professional work of person who is
not member of ICAI,
Provided that nothing shall prohibit member 'from entering into profit sharing or other similar
arrangements, including receiving any share, commission or brokerage in the fees, with member of
such professional body or other person having qualifications, as is referred in item (2) of this part
Referral fees: CAiP allowed to charge Referral Fees from another CAIP for referring client to him.
Note: Accepting commission from regd valuer for referring valuation assignments → Guilty
Clause (4):
• enters into partnership, in or outside India, with any person other than
CAIP or such person who is member of professional body having prescribed qualifications,
including a resident who but for his residence abroad would be entitled to be regd. as member
under section $4(1)(v)$ or
• whose qualifications are recognized by CG or Council for permitting such partnerships.
Note: Same qualifications like clause (2) except MBA.
Clause (5) Secures either through services of a person who is not an employee of such CA or who
not his partner or by means which are not open to a CA, any professional business. Provided that
nothing herein contained shall be construed as prohibiting any agreement permitted in terms of
item (2), (3) and (4) of this part.
Clause (6) Solicits clients or professional work directly or indirectly by circular, advertisement,
personal communication or interview or any other means.
K
Provided nothing shall prevent or prohibit-
SARI
Any CA from securing/applying/requesting/inviting professional work from another CAIP or
A member from responding to tenders/enquiries by users of professional services or organizations
& securing professional work as a consequence.
Exception: As per Council guidelines, CAIP shall respond to tender in areas of services exclusively
reserved for CAs, such as audit & attestation services only when minimum fees is prescribed in
tender document.

02 Professional Ethics
L
☑
Application for empanelment for allotment of audit & other professional work: Govt Dept &
institutions maintain panel of CAs for allotment of professional work. Where existence of
panel known to CA, free to write to concerned org. Not proper to make roving enquiries.
Permissible to quote fees on enquiries received from bodies, which maintain such panel.
Publication of Books, Articles or Presentation: Not permissible to mention profess
BAR
attainment(s), of member or firm. May indicate the designation "CA" & name of firm.,
Issue of Greeting Cards or Invitations: Designation "CA" & firm name may be used in greeting co
invitations for marriages & religious ceremonies & for opening or inauguration of office, chang
office premises & change in telephone nos., provided invitations sent only to clients, relative
friends of members concerned.
Advertisement for Silver, Golden, Platinum or Centenary celebrations of CA Firms may be publis
in newspaper or newsletter. [What about opening ceremony?
Sponsoring Activities
NO
]
A CAIP or CA Firm not permitted to sponsor an event. May sponsor event conducted by a Program
Organizing Unit (PoU) of ICAI, with prior approval of Continuing Professional Education (C
Directorate of ICAI.
Members sponsoring activities relating to CSR may mention their name with prefix "CA". Howe-
Firm name or CA Logo hot permitted.  Name permitted Bock, Anticles/finvitation)
Sharing Firm Profile with prospective Client: Not permitted to share Firm profile with prospec-
Client unless in response to a proposed client's specific query.
Television or Movie Credits: Exhibition of name is not made differently as compared to ot
entries in the credits.
Soliciting professional work by making roving enquiries: Not permissible to address letters, email
Circulars to persons likely to require services of CA since it would tantamount to advertisement.
Seeking work from Professional Colleagues: Issue of advertisement or circular by CA, seeking  
om professional colleagues would be in violation. SARI is allowed from another CAIP.
cope of representation which auditor is entitled to make u/s 140(4) of Companies Act, 20
oportunity not being abused to secure needless publicity. Letter should be set out in dignit
Inner how he has been acting independently & conscientiously through the term of office
dition, indicate his willingness to continue as auditor if reappointed by shareholders.

02 Professional Ethics
ics
Acceptance of original professional work by a member emanating from client introduced to him by
another member: Member not accept original professional work emanating from client introduced to
him by another member. If any professional work of such client comes directly, it should be his duty
to ask client to come through other member dealing generally with his original work.
Podcastt News changels
Audit
Giving Public Interviews: Not result in publicity. Details about members or firms not given in a
manner highlighting professional attainments. Detail given as response to specific question & factual
nature only.
Prohibited from inserting advertisements under box numbers in newspapers.
In Educational Videos, No reference made to CAs Firm wherein member is a partner/proprietor.
Not contain contact details or website address
Website of CA Firms (Council Guidelines)

 Should be on Pull model instead of Push model

02 Professional Ethics
S
Inserted on behalf of client for staff for their office or acquisition or disposition of property
For sale of business or property by member acting in prof capacity as trustee, liquidator or
receiver (litre)
Note: When advertising for staff its desirable to avoid saying "Well known firm".
Examples:
1. Mr. S. a CAiP published book & gave his personal details including professional experience as author.
Mr. S being a CAiP has committed professional misconduct by mentioning his professional experience.
2. M/s XYZ, firm of CAs created website "www.xyzindia.com". Website besides containing details of
firm and bio-data of partners also contains passport size photographs of all partners of firm.
CA firm can create its own website using any format. However, website should be so designed that it
does not solicit clients or professional work & not amount to direct or indirect advertisement. ICAI
Guidelines allow firm share firm details, bio-data of partners & passport size photo.
Conclusion: Not Guilty
3. M/s LMN, firm of CAs responded to tender from SG for computerization of land revenue records.
For this purpose, firm also paid 50,000 as earnest deposit as part of terms of tender.
Matter relates to services other than audit, members can respond to any tender. Further, for non-
exclusive area, members permitted to pay reasonable amount towards earnest money/security deposits.
Conclusion: Not Guilty
4. Mr. Honest, CAIP, wrote two letters to $M/s$ XY CA firm requesting them to allot him some
professional work. He also wrote letter to $M/s$ ABC, CA firm for securing professional work. Mr. Clever,
another CA, informed ICAI regarding Mr. Honest's approach to secure professional work.
Es Mr. Honest wrong in soliciting professional work?
 ) Nothing shall prevent or prohibit any CA from applying or requesting for or inviting or securing
professional work from another CAIP.
) Such restraint has been put so that members maintain their independence & command respect from
prospective clients.
Conclusion: Not guilty
Clause (7)
Advertises his professional attainments or services or
uses any designation/expressions other than CA on professional documents, visiting cards, letter
neads or sign boards
 unless it is degree of a University established by law in India or recognized by CG or titl
indicating ICAI membership or of any other institution recognized by CG or Council.
Member in practice may advertise through a write up, setting out service provided by him or fir
 particulars of firm subject to such council guidelines.

as CS &/or CMA but not use designation/s of those Institute/s simultaneously with "CA".

member treated as a member in full-time practice.

]
Practice as Advocate : CAiP eligible to practise as advocates subject to permission of Bar Council should
not use designation 'Chartered Accountant' for matters involving practice as advocate.
For other matters they should use designation 'Chartered Accountant' but not use designation 'CA' &
'Advocate' simultaneously.
Practice as Company Secretary/Cost Management Accountant: CAiP otherwise eligible may also practice
as CS &/or CMA but not use designation/s of those Institute/s simultaneously with "CA".
Note: If permission being granted to a CAiP to also hold COP of sister Institute(s)/Bar Council, such
member treated as a member in full-time practice.
Notice in Press relating to Success in Examination: Should not contain any undesirable publicity.
Candidate's name, address, school & local background, examination passed with details of prize or
place gained, name of principal, firm & town in which principal practices may be published.
Reports & Certificates: Manner of publication limited to enable report or certificate to serve its
proper purpose. Use letterhead for issuing reports & certificates.
Appearance of CAs on Electronic Media (including Internet): May appear on television, films,
Internet & Radio or give lectures at forums & may give their names & describe themselves as CAs.
Special qualifications or knowledge relevant to subject matter may be given. Firm name may be
mentioned; however, exaggerated claim/comparison not permissible. Must not be promotion of him/
rm but an objective professional view of topic. Mention of membership of ICAI is desirable to
chieve suitable publicity for ICAI.

s when acting in their capacity as CAS. Reference to professional firm not to be given.

02 Professional Ethics
rganising Training Courses, Seminars etc. for his staff: May invite staff of other CAs & clients.
Indue prominence should not be given to name of CA in any booklet or document.
 riting Articles/Letters (connected with profession) to Press: May give their names & use CA.
ize of Sign Board: Use of glow signs or lights on large sized boards not permissible.
ublic Announcements with details of CA as Directors:
rospectus or public announcements shouldn't publish descriptions about CA's expertise,
pecialisation & knowledge. [Both 6 & 7 apply]
 should be approved by him.
Member should invite attention of mgt to provisions & request that before communication is issued,
Ise of expression 'CA' is permissible. Directorships held by member in other Companies can be
given, but name of firm should not be given.
Use of logo/monogram of any kind/form/style/design/colour etc. is prohibited. Only use CA logo.
Printing QR code on visiting card allowed giving name/address/contact details/firm name
Notes:
When CA while delivering speech at Conference talks about his expertise & services of firm & requests
audience to approach him guilty under clause 6 & 7.
1. A CAIP uses visiting card in which he designates himself, besides as CA, as a Tax Consultant.
Use sec 7 with clause 7 → Guilty
2. B, a CAiP is partner in 3 firms. While printing personal letter heads, gave names of all firms in which
he is a partner → Not guilty (can't mention date of setting up practice)
3. Offer document of listed Co. in which Mr. D (CAIP) is director mentions name of Mr. D as a director
with his various professional attainments & spheres of specialisation.
Council stated that if public Co, in which CAiP is director, issues prospectus or gives announcement that
gives descriptions about CA's expertise, specialisation and knowledge in any particular field, it shall be
a misconduct under Clauses (6) & (7) Part I First Schedule to CA Act, 1949.
Council further stated that in such cases member has to take necessary steps to ensure that such
prospectus or public announcements don't advertise his professional attainments & don't directly or
indirectly amount to solicitation of clients for professional work by the members.
Conclusion: Guilty
4. A CAIP, empanelled Insolvency Professional (IP) mentioned this on his visiting cards, letter heads &
other communications. Person residing in his neighbourhood, filed a complaint for professional
misconduct for such mention of IP → Complaint is not valid (this is title recognised by CG).

02 Professional Ethics
Organising Training Courses, Seminars etc. for his staff: May invite staff of other CAs & clients.
Undue prominence should not be given to name of CA in any booklet or document.
Writing Articles/Letters (connected with profession) to Press: May give their names & use CA.
Size of Sign Board: Use of glow signs or lights on large sized boards not permissible.
Public Announcements with details of CA as Directors:
Prospectus or public announcements shouldn't publish descriptions about CA's expertise,
specialisation & knowledge. [Both 6 & 7 apply]
Member should invite attention of mgt to provisions & request that before communication is issued,
it should be approved by him.
Use of expression 'CA' is permissible. Directorships held by member in other Companies can be
given, but name of firm should not be given.
Use of logo/monogram of any kind/form/style/design/colour etc. is prohibited. Only use CA logo.
Printing QR code on visiting card allowed giving name/address/contact details/firm name
Notes:
When CA while delivering speech at Conference talks about his expertise & services of firm & requests
audience to approach him quilty under clause 6 & 7.
1. A CAIP uses visiting card in which he designates himself, besides as CA, as a Tax Consultant.
Use sec 7 with clause 7 → Guilty
2. B, a CAIP is partner in 3 firms. While printing personal letter heads, gave names of all firms in which
he is a partner → Not guilty (can't mention date of setting up practice)
3. Offer document of listed Co. in which Mr. D (CAIP) is director mentions name of Mr. D as a director
with his various professional attainments & spheres of specialisation.
Council stated that if public Co, in which CAiP is director, issues prospectus or gives announcement that
gives descriptions about CA's expertise, specialisation and knowledge in any particular field, it shall be
a misconduct under Clauses (6) & (7) Part I First Schedule to CA Act, 1949.
Council further stated that in such cases member has to take necessary steps to ensure that such
prospectus or public announcements don't advertise his professional attainments & don't directly or
indirectly amount to solicitation of clients for professional work by the members.
Conclusion: Guilty
4. A CAIP, empanelled Insolvency Professional (IP) mentioned this on his visiting cards, letter heads &
other communications. Person residing in his neighbourhood, filed a complaint for professional
misconduct for such mention of IP → Complaint is not valid (this is title recognised by CG).

Professional reasons for not accepting an audit:
a. Non-compliance of Sec 139 & 140 of Companies Act, 2013
b. Non-payment of undisputed Audit Fees by auditees other than in case of Sick Units
Where Previous Auditor not available for accepting payment & not possible to transfer electron
Advise client to purchase Demand Draft of undisputed Audit Fees, may accept Audit after verifie
c. Issuance of qualified report*
*may accept audit if satisfied that attitude of retiring auditor was not proper & justified.
If he feels that retiring auditor qualified report for good & valid reasons, refuse to accept a
There is no rule which would prevent auditor from accepting appointment offered in
circumstances. Before accepting audit, ascertain full facts of case.
Correct procedure when client wants to change his auditor & wants you to take up his work?
Co. should be asked whether retiring auditor been informed of intention to change.
If answer is 'Yes', then communicate with retiring auditor.
If old auditor hasn't been informed & client not willing to inform, ask reason for proposed c
If no valid reason, it would be healthy practice to not accept audit.
If he decides to accept audit he should communicate with retiring auditor.
Note: Sometimes, retiring auditor fails to respond to communication of incoming auditor.
Auditor appointed can act, after waiting for a reasonable time for a reply.
Members should retain positive evidence of delivery of communication to addressee.
Following would provide such evidence:
Post
Communication by a letter sent through "Registered Acknowledgement due", or
By hand against a written acknowledgement, or
Acknowledgement of communication from retiring auditor's vide email address regd with IC
ast known official email address, or
nique Identification Number (UDIN) generated on UDIN portal (guidelines to be issued)
Letters posted under Certificate of Posting not considered valid (No positive evidence of deliv
ecial Cases:
emises found Locked: Deemed to be delivered to retiring auditor.
m not found at Regd address: If address is same as regd with ICAI on date of dispatch,
be deemed to be delivered, unless retiring auditor proves it was not really served.

☑ Ascertain whether any circumstances which warrant him not to accept appointment.
Professional reasons for not accepting an audit:
a. Non-compliance of Sec 139 & 140 of Companies Act, 2013
b. Non-payment of undisputed Audit Fees by auditees other than in case of Sick Units
Advise client to purchase Demand Draft of undisputed Audit Fees, may accept Audit after verification.
Where Previous Auditor not available for accepting payment & not possible to transfer electronically
c. Issuance of qualified report*
☐
*may accept audit if satisfied that attitude of retiring auditor was not proper & justified.
There is no rule which would prevent auditor from accepting appointment offered in these
If he feels that retiring auditor qualified report for good & valid reasons, refuse to accept audit.
circumstances. Before accepting audit, ascertain full facts of case.
Correct procedure when client wants to change his auditor & wants you to take up his work?
Co. should be asked whether retiring auditor been informed of intention to change.
• If answer is 'Yes', then communicate with retiring auditor.
• If old auditor hasn't been informed & client not willing to inform, ask reason for proposed change.
• If no valid reason, it would be healthy practice to not accept audit.
• If he decides to accept audit he should communicate with retiring auditor.
Note: Sometimes, retiring auditor fails to respond to communication of incoming auditor.
Auditor appointed can act, after waiting for a reasonable time for a reply.
Members should retain positive evidence of delivery of communication to addressee.
Following would provide such evidence:
Post
a) Communication by a letter sent through "Registered Acknowledgement due", or
b) By hand against a written acknowledgement, or
c) Acknowledgement of communication from retiring auditor's vide email address regd with ICAI or
last known official email address, or
d) Unique Identification Number (UDIN) generated on UDIN portal (guidelines to be issued)
*Letters posted under Certificate of Posting not considered valid (No positive evidence of delivery)
Special Cases:
Premises found Locked: Deemed to be delivered to retiring auditor.
Firm not found at Regd address : If address is same as regd with ICAI on date of dispatch, letter
will be deemed to be delivered, unless retiring auditor proves it was not really served.

02 Professional Ethics
nt audit with earlier auditor: Necessary for new auditor appointed to act jointly with earlier
itor to communicate with such earlier auditor.
ecial Audit under Income Tax Act, 1961: Healthy practice if Tax Auditor conducting spl. audit
ler Income Tax Act, 1961 communicates Statutory Auditor.
uncil guidelines:
Compa
quirement for communicating with previous auditor being CAIP would apply to all types of Audit
Statutory Audit, Tax Audit, Internal Audit, Concurrent Audit or any other kind of audit.
mmunication in case of Assignments done by other professionals: For assignments done by other
ofessionals not being CAs, it would be a healthy practice to communicate.
ck of time in acceptance of Government Audits: If no time to wait for reply from outgoing auditor,
coming auditor may give conditional acceptance of appointment & commence work.
acceptance letter, make clear to client that acceptance is subject to professional objections,
om previous auditors & he'll decide about final acceptance after reply of previous auditor.
pplicable for audit of Govt cos./banks or their branches)
r. X, a CA accepted appointment as tax auditor of a firm u/s 44AB, of Income-tax Act, &
mmenced tax audit within 2 days of appointment since client was in a hurry to file Tax Return.
fter commencing, Mr. X realised his mistake of accepting tax audit without sending any
ommunication to previous tax auditor. To rectify his mistake, before signing tax audit report, he
ent a registered post to previous auditor & obtained the postal acknowledgement.
Will Mr. X be held guilty under CA Act?
s per Clause $8/1/1$ to CA Act, 1949, Mr. X will be held guilty since he has accepted tax audit
ithout first communicating with previous auditor in writing.
- Object is to ascertain whether any circumstances to not accept appointment, for eg, previou
auditor changed on account of having qualified report or did not continue due to somethin
wrong with administration of business.
Retiring auditor may even give out info. regarding condition of accounts of client or reason tha
impelled him to qualify his report.
Under all circumstances, it's essential for incoming auditor to carefully consider facts befor
deciding whether or not he should accept the audit.
As a matter of professional courtesy & professional obligation it's necessary for new auditor
communicate with earlier auditor.
onclusion: Mr. X will be held guilty of professional misconduct.
W, a Chartered Accountant had sent letters under certificate of posting to previous audi
forming him his appointment as an auditor before commencement of audit by him  Guilt

02 Professional Ethics
Toint audit with earlier auditor: Necessary for new auditor appointed to act jointly with earlier
uditor to communicate with such earlier auditor.
Special Audit under Income Tax Act, 1961: Healthy practice if Tax Auditor conducting spl. audit
inder Income Tax Act, 1961 communicates Statutory Auditor.
Council guidelines:
Requirement for communicating with previous auditor being CAIP would apply to all types of Audit
viz., Statutory Audit, Tax Audit, Internal Audit, Concurrent Audit or any other kind of audit.
Communication in case of Assignments done by other professionals: For assignments done by other
professionals not being CAs, it would be a healthy practice to communicate.
Lack of time in acceptance of Government Audits: If no time to wait for reply from outgoing auditor,
incoming auditor may give conditional acceptance of appointment & commence work.
In acceptance letter, make clear to client that acceptance is subject to professional objections,
from previous auditors & he'll decide about final acceptance after reply of previous auditor.
(Applicable for audit of Govt cos./banks or their branches)
Mr. X, a CA accepted appointment as tax auditor of a firm u/s 44AB, of Income-tax Act, &
commenced tax audit within 2 days of appointment since client was in a hurry to file Tax Return.
After commencing, Mr. X realised his mistake of accepting tax audit without sending any
communication to previous tax auditor. To rectify his mistake, before signing tax audit report, he
sent a registered post to previous auditor & obtained the postal acknowledgement.
Will Mr. X be held quilty under CA Act?
As per Clause $8/1/1$ to CA Act, 1949, Mr. X will be held guilty since he has accepted tax audit,
without first communicating with previous auditor in writing.
✓ Object is to ascertain whether any circumstances to not accept appointment, for eg, previous
auditor changed on account of having qualified report or did not continue due to somethin
wrong with administration of business.
✓ Retiring auditor may even give out info. regarding condition of accounts of client or reason tha
impelled him to qualify his report.
✓ Under all circumstances, it's essential for incoming auditor to carefully consider facts befor
deciding whether or not he should accept the audit.
✓ As a matter of professional courtesy & professional obligation it's necessary for new auditor
communicate with earlier auditor.
Conclusion: Mr. X will be held guilty of professional misconduct.
2. W, a Chartered Accountant had sent letters under certificate of posting to previous audit
informing him his appointment as an auditor before commencement of audit by him  Guilty

Clause (9) accepts appointment as auditor of a Co. without first ascertaining whether requirements of
Sec 139 & 140 of Cos. Act, 2013, for such appointment have been duly complied with.
Obligatory for incoming auditor to ascertain from Co. that appropriate procedure for his appointment

✓ Not sufficient to accept certificate from mgt that above sections have been complied with.
☑ Necessary to verify relevant records of Co. & whether Co. has complied with above sections.
☑ If Co. not willing to allow auditor to verify relevant records, should not accept audit assignment.
"Note: Do revise Sec 139 & 140"
Procedure if Co. doesn't want to reappoint retiring auditor:
Members send special notice u/s 140(4) (not before 3 months but atleast 14 days before GM) for
resolution at AGM. Also required to remove auditor before expiry of term.
Co. send a copy to retiring auditor. Also send to members at least 7 days before meeting.
Retiring auditor shall submit representation to Co. to be sent to members.
Records to verify:
ESB Guidelines for removal/resignation by Auditor
A. Auditor willing for reappointment but not reappointed  file with ICAI a copy of statement which
is also sent to shareholders by mgt of Co (Obligatory for incoming auditor to obtain such copy from
BOD & consider before accepting audit)
B. Auditor resigns → send communication to BOD + ICAI stating professional reasons
(Obligatory for incoming auditor to obtain such copy from BOD & consider before accepting audit)
C. ESB can ask for add. info if required
D. Also applicable to removal of auditor by govt/other statutory bodies.
CA Raja was appointed as Auditor of Castle Ltd for year 2023-24. Since he declined to accept
appointment, BOD appointed CA Rani as auditor in place of Raja, which was also accepted by CA Rani.
Board can appoint auditor in case of casual vacancy u/s 139(8) of Companies Act, 2013. Non-acceptance
of appointment by CA. Raja does not constitute a casual vacancy. It will be deemed that no auditor was

Specific Resolution May engage in following categories of business/ occupations, after obtaining
specific & prior approval of Council in each case:
Employment in business concerns provided member and/or his relatives do not hold "substantial
interest" in such concerns. (20% or more)
Full-time or part-time employment in non-business concern.
Ο Office of MD or a WTD of body corporate provided member and/or any of his relatives don't hold
substantial interest in such concern
Interest in family business concerns or concerns in which interest has been acquired as a result of
relationships & in management of which no active part is taken.
Interest in an educational institution.
Part-time or full-time lectureship for courses other than relating to ICAI's exams conducted under
auspices of ICAI or Regional councils or their branches.
Part-time or full-time tutorship under any educational institution other than ICAI coaching
organization.
Editorship of journals other than professional journals.
Any other business/occupation for which Executive Committee considers permission may be granted.

caching hours shouldn't exceed 25 hrs a week to undertake attest functions.
ading in commodity derivates treated as business.

Valuation of papers, acting as paper-setter, head-examiner or a moderator, for any examinat
Editorship of professional journals (Eg Company Audit Journal) like GST mey apun karena
Surveyor & Loss Assessor under the Insurance Act, 1938
Recovery consultant in banking sector
Owning agricultural land & carrying out agricultural activity
Specific Resolution - May engage in following categories of business/ occupations, after ob-
pecific & prior approval of Council in each case:
mployment in business concerns provided member and/or his relatives do not hold "subs-
terest" in such concerns. (20% or more)
ull-time or part-time employment in non-business concern.
Ffice of MD or a WTD of body corporate provided member and/or any of his relatives don
Ostantial interest in such concern
terest in family business concerns or concerns in which interest has been acquired as a res
ationships & in management of which no active part is taken.
erest in an educational institution.
-time or full-time lectureship for courses other than relating to ICAI's exams conducted
pices of ICAI or Regional councils or their branches.
-time or full-time tutorship under any educational institution other than ICAI coc
nization.
orship of journals other than professional journals.
ther business/occupation for which Executive Committee considers permission may be gro
for member to be promoter / signatory to Memorandum & Articles of Association of Co
for such promoter / signatory to be Director Simplicitor of that Co.
ng hours shouldn't exceed 25 hrs a week to undertake attest functions.
in commodity derivates treated as business.

With

02 Professional Ethic
Q. Whether auditor of a Subsidiary can be a Director of its Holding Co.?
Ethical Standard Board decided that auditor of Subsidiary Co. can't be a Director of its Holding Co.,
as it will affect independence of an auditor. Public conscience needs to be kept ahead of the law.
Member in practice in a HUF doing business
Specitiic
Case Approos
requi
CAIP can acquire interest in family business in any of following manner:
Proprietary/partnership firm/ HUF as its Karta or a member
Necessary to provide evidence that interest in family business devolved on him as a result of
inheritance/succession/partition of family business.
Show that he was not actively engaged in business & family business concern not created by him.
Furnish a declaration & documents to concerned Decentralized Office.
CAIP engaged as Karta of HUF will be within limit prescribed by Council if he makes investments
from funds of HUF only & not actively engaged in mgt of said business.
1. A CA holding COP and having 4 articled clerks under him accepts appointment as full-time lecture
in college. Also, becomes a partner with his brother in a business.
Key Points: Council formulated Regulations 190A & 191 to provide a basis for considering application
of CAS seeking permission to engage in other business or occupation.
A member can accept full time lecturer-ship in a college only after obtaining specific & pria
approval of Council as also becoming a partner in a business would require specific permission.
Conclusion: Guilty
2. Mr. A, a CAIP, took over as executive chairman of Software Co. on 1.4.2024. On 10.4.2024  
applied to Council for permission.
Key Points: Mr A was engaged in other occupation between 01.04.2024 & 10.04.24, witho
permission of Council.
Conclusion: Guilty
3. CA Moksh, a leading income tax practitioner based in Mumbai with exceptional writing skills, al
serves as editor of a non-chartered accountancy-related journal. He devotes approximately 50
of his time to managing journal's editorial responsibilities. Is CA Moksh guilty?
Conclusion: Specific permission of ICAI to be obtained otherwise deemed to be guilty
professional misconduct
☑
Clause (12) Allows a person not being a member of institute in practice or
✓ a member not being his partner to
☑ sign on his behalf or his firm, any balance sheet, profit and loss account, report or FS.
Who can sign?
CA iP & Partner

02 Professional Ethics
Exceptions:
Council clarified that power to sign routine documents on which professional opinion or authent
is not required may be delegated in following instances:
Issue of audit queries during audit.
Asking for info. or issue of questionnaire.
Letter forwarding draft observations/FS.
Initiating & stamping of vouchers & schedules prepared for audit.
Acknowledging & carrying on routine correspondence with clients. Email
Issue of memorandum of cash verification & other physical verification or recording results in t
Issuing acknowledgements for records produced.
Raising of bills & issuing acknowledgements for money receipts.
Attending routine matters in tax practice, subject to provisions of Sec 288 of Income Tax Ac
Any other routine matter.

Adornment Partner busu Arick
 , Authority delegated by CA But Authority not used is not a defence for CA → Prof misconduct

This clause read with Sec 26 of CA Act, 1949 stipulates that no person other than member of I
shall sign any document on behalf of a CAiP or a firm of CAs in his or its professional capacity
➤ Term 'FS' would cover examination of accounts or FS.
A
Further, Clause (1) of Part II of Second Schedule to CĄ Act, 1949 states that member of IC
whether in practice or not, shall be deemed to be, guilty of professional misconduct, if
contravenes any of provisions of this Act or regulations or guidelines issued by Council.
➤ Conclusion: Guilty under both clauses.
2. CA. Smart, a CAiP was on Europe tour between 15-9-24 and 25-9-24. On 18-9-24 a message
received from one of his clients requesting for stock certificate to be produced to bank on or befo
20-9-24. Due to urgency, CA. Smart directed his assistant, who is also a CA, to sign and issue sto
certificate after due verification, on his behalf.
Conclusion: Guilty

02 Professional Ethics
PART II - Professional misconduct in relation to members of the Institute inservice
Elees
A member of Institute (other than a member in practice) shall be deemed to be guilty of professional
misconduct, if he being an employee of any Co., firm or person:
Clause (1) pays or allows or agrees to pay directly or indirectly to any person any share in
emoluments of employment undertaken by him.
Can share with relatives, dependents, friends, if not a consideration for procuring/retaining a job.
Job must be procured & retained with own professional capabilities & not by any financial deal
impairing professional dignity.
FPG
Clause (2) accepts or agrees to accept any part of fees, profits or gains from a lawyer, a chartered
accountant or broker engaged by such Co., firm or person or agent or customer of such Co., firm
or person by way of commission or gratification eder

PART IV- Other misconduct in relation to members of the Institute generally
Member of ICAI, whether in practice or not, deemed to be guilty of other misconduct, if he
Clause (1) is held guilty by any civil or criminal court for offence punishable with imprisonmen-
term not exceeding six months $[<=6$ months]
Clause (2) in opinion of Council,
✓
brings disrepute to profession or Institute
>6mon part-  se Cond Schedule.
as a result of his action whether or not related to his professional work.
CA is expected to maintain highest standards of integrity even in his personal affairs & any dev
from these standards, even in non-professional work, would expose him to disciplinary action.
Note: Before starting any ans. of this clause ICAI gives this line → Section 21 of the Char-
Accountants Act, 1949 provides that a member is liable for disciplinary action if he is guilty o-
professional or "Other Misconduct."
Examples, where a member may be found guilty of "Other Misconduct":
Retains books of $a/c$ and documents of client & fails to return without reasonable cause.
Uses the services of his articled or audit assistant for purposes other than professional prac
[Eg. Using them for election campaigning]  Ramukaka ( )   fund
singafde
 Misappropriation of money by office-bearer of Regional Council of ICAI for personal use.
- Not replying within reasonable time to letter of public authorities [Eg Police Station]
Where CA had adopted coercive methods on a bank for having a loan sanctioned to him.
majboor
YKS & Co., a firm of CAs, was appointed as concurrent auditor of a bank. YKS, proprietor, used
influence to get a loan & thereafter failed to repay the loan → Guilty
Cheque returned Unpaid with Remark "Refer to Drawer"
Negotiable Instruments Act 1881, where any cheque is returned by bank unpaid, either
insufficiency of funds, drawer shall be deemed to have committed an offence.
Remark "refer to drawer" need not necessarily be only due to insufficiency of funds.
If it is proved that cheques were dishonoured due to insufficiency of funds, then CA would be H
guilty of "other misconduct".

The Second Schedule
Part I - Professional Misconduct in relation to CAIP
A CAIP shall be deemed to be guilty of professional misconduct, if he
Confidentiality
Clause (1) Discloses info. acquired in his professional engg. to any person other than
his client so engaging him without the consent of his client or
otherwise than as required by any law for the time being in force.
Exceptions:
Disclosure allowed only with consent of client or as part of professional duties (Eg submitting info
to Exchange Control Authorities)
No misconduct in case of legal compulsion as required by Evidence Act
Sec 143(12) - Reporting of fraud to CG within 60 days
Eg. Mr. P, a CA was invited by Chamber of Commerce to present a paper on issues facing Indian Leather
Industry. During his presentation he shared some of vital info. of client's business under impression
That it will help Nation to compete with other countries at international level..
Duty of confidentiality continues even after completion of assignment. CA may however, disclose info.
In case it is required for performance of his professional duties.
Conclusion: Professional Misconduct

02 Professional Ethics
Clause (7) doesn't exercise due diligence or is grossly negligent in conduct of his professional duties.
honestly & reasonably_discharged his duties.
It is a vital clause which gets attracted whenever it's necessary to judge whether accountant has
Expression negligence covers wide field from frontiers of fraud to collateral minor negligence.
Examples:
high frontiers
Conducted Stock audit without visiting the site, relied on mgt reports
miner  small+low 
Issued certificate of consumption of Raw material based on minutes of BOD guilty clause 2 & 7
of this schedule
Not submitted his report in due time to enable Co to comply with Statutory requirement
Failed to check a forged signature which he could have checked
Shared password of his digital signature certificate with client  Guilty
minos
Certified circulation figures of newspaper based on MIS report without examining books of $a/c$
1. CA C conducted statutory audit of a Haryana daily 'New Era' certified the circulation figures based
on Management Info. System Report (M.I.S Report) without examining the books of A $A/c.$
To check no. of paid copies verification of remittances from agents, credit allowed to
for unsold
copies returned, examination of books of $a/c$ is essential.
Certification based on statistical info. without verifying financial records amounts to gross negligence
& failure to exercise due diligence.
Conclusion: Guilty
Clause (8): Fails to obtain sufficient info. necessary for expression of an opinion or its exceptions an
sufficiently material to negate the expression of an opinion.
Examples:
Transaction took place between ABC Firm & R developers but reported in books of A
Construction. Loan amount was material. Guilty under clause 6, 7 & 8 of Part I of Second Sched
CA audited books of A Itd that had investment of Rs 10L, later it was found real value was 25k →
guilty under clause 2, 7, 8 of Part I of Second Schedule of CA Act, 1949
Certificate of circulation of Periodical $w/o$ verifying underlying record bank statements, printer
sales records etc → Guilty under Clause 8 [can add (7) too]
Non-Compliance
Clause (9) Fails to invite attention to any material departure from the generally accepted proc
of audit applicable to the circumstances
Generally accepted audit procedure => Engg. & Quality Control Standards, Statements, G
Clarifications, Guidance Notes Technical Guides, Practice Manuals, Studies and Other Paper-

02 Professional Ethics
Clause (7) doesn't exercise due diligence or is grossly negligent in conduct of his professional duties.
honestly & reasonably discharged his duties.
It is a vital clause which gets attracted whenever it's necessary to judge whether accountant has
Expression negligence covers wide field from frontiers of fraud to collateral minor negligence.
Examples:
high +frontier!
Conducted Stock audit without visiting the site, relied on mgt reports
Issued certificate of consumption of Raw material based on minutes of BOD guilty clause 2 & 7
of this schedule
minh small+low+
Not submitted his report in due time to enable Co to comply with Statutory requirement
Failed to check a forged signature which he could have checked
Shared password of his digital signature certificate with client Guilty
minos
Certified circulation figures of newspaper based on MIS report without examining books of $a/c$
1. CA C conducted statutory audit of a Haryana daily 'New Era' certified the circulation figures based
on Management Info. System Report (M.I.S Report) without examining the books of $A/c$.
To check no. of paid copies verification of remittances from agents, credit allowed to agents for unsold
copies returned, examination of books of $a/c$ is essential.
Certification based on statistical info, without verifying financial records amounts to gross negligence
& failure to exercise due diligence.
Conclusion: Guilty
Clause (8): Fails to obtain sufficient info. necessary for expression of an opinion or its exceptions ar
sufficiently material to negate the expression of an opinion.
Examples:
 Transaction took place between ABC Firm & R developers but reported in books of Al
Construction. Loan amount was material. Guilty under clause 6, 7 & 8 of Part I of Second Schedu
 CA audited books of A Itd that had investment of Rs 10L, later it was found real value was 25k →
guilty under clause 2, 7, 8 of Part I of Second Schedule of CA Act,1949
 Certificate of circulation of Periodical $w/o$ verifying underlying record bank statements, printer b
sales records etc → Guilty under Clause 8 [can add (7) too]
Non-Compliance
Clause (9) Fails to invite attention to any material departure from the generally accepted proce
of audit applicable to the circumstances
Generally accepted audit procedure => Engg. & Quality Control Standards, Statements, Ge
Clarifications, Guidance Notes Technical Guides, Practice Manuals, Studies and Other Papers

C
N
Auf
02 Professional Ethics
Special Points:
Audit of listed Cos: Done by Auditor subject to Peer Review process of ICAI & hold valid certa.
issued by Peer Review Board of ICAI.
Firm Reg No. & Membership No. to be mentioned on reports of attestation engg,
UDIN is mandatory to be generated for all kinds of certifications & audit reports
Clause (10) fails to keep moneys of his client other than fees remuneration/money to be experdos.
in a separate banking a/c or
to use such moneys for intended purposes within a reasonable time.
Spl points:
Advance received against services excluded from scope,
Money recd for expenses to be incurred in reasonably short time not to be deposited in bank al
Money recd in capacity of trustee, executor liquidator, etc keep in separate bank a/c
A charitable institution entrusted ₹10L with its auditors M/s R & Co., a CA firm, to invest in specifi
securities. Auditors deposited it in their Savings bank a/c & no investment was made in next 3 months
Clause 10/1/2 +
In course of his engagement, a member may be entrusted with client's money. If he receive such furcs
it is his duty to deposit them in a separate banking a/c & utilise such funds only as per instructions
client or for purposes intended by the client.
Conclusion: Guilty of professional misconduct.
PART II - Professional misconduct in relation to members of the Institute generally
A member of the Institute, whether in practice or not, shall be deemed to be guilty of professional
misconduct, if he -
Clause (1) contravenes any of provisions of this Act or regulations or any guidelines issued by Council
Examples:
CA certified in Form K-2 audit clerk in service with him, article employed elsewhere 11-5 pm & then
come to office work till 8 pm.
Took article intern under him even when no vacancy was there, Articleship deed not regd.
Took loan from firm where article & his father were interested
Didn't pay stipend as per Reg 48 to article, only paid when article left. Said he had agreement to
pay fees annually → held guilty
CA without a COP issued a certificate of raw material consumption guilty u/s 6 of CA Act, 1949
ause (2) being an employee of any Co., firm or person,
discloses confidential info. acquired in course of his employment
except when required by any law or permitted by employer.

Part III - Other misconduct in relation to members of the Institute generally
Member of Institute, whether in practice or not, deemed to be guilty of other misconduct, if held
guilty by any civil/criminal court for offence punishable with imprisonment for a term > 6 months.
Council General Guidelines (Final Stage of Chapter)
Chapter I: Applicable to all Members of ICAI whether in practice or not.
Chapter II: A member of ICAI who is an employee shall exercise due diligence & shall not be grossly
negligent in conduct of his duties. Clause 77 Part I of second sch... 7/1/2

less than the limits prescribed u/s 44AA of the Income Tax Act, 1961 → Guilty

Chapter VI: Tax Audit assignments under Section 44 AB of the Income-tax Act, 1961
CAIP shall not accept, in a financial year, more than 60 tax audit assignments u/s 44AB of Income tax
Act, 1961.
If there are 10 partners in a firm, then all partners of firm can collectively sign 600 tax audit reports

02 Professional Ethics
Audits u/s 44AD, 44ADA, & 44AE of IT Act 1961 not counted
. Audit of HO & Branch office counted as 1 assignment
Audit of More than 1 branch of same concern $=1$ assignment
Part time practicing partner not allowed to accept tax audit assignments.
Maintain record of tax audit assignments accepted in each Ay in format prescribed by Council.
Mr. G is a partner in $M/s$. XYZ & Co., CA firm with 6 partners. During AY 2023-24, Mr. G alor
signed 290 tax audit reports consisting of both corporate & non-corporate assesses.
• In given case, there are 6 partners in $M/s$ XYZ & Co., accordingly, specified ceiling limit for fir
be (60 tax audit assignments per partner $X~6)=360$
All 6 partners can collectively sign 360 tax audit reports.
These 360 tax audits may be distributed between partners in any manner.
Conclusion: Hence, Mr. G shall not be deemed to guilty of professional misconduct provided tot
of tax audit reports on behalf of firm do not exceeds 360.
Soath.
Chapter VII: Appointment of an Auditor in case of non-payment of undisputed fees
A CAIP shall not accept appointment as auditor of entity in case undisputed audit fee of anothe
for carrying out statutory audit under Cos. Act, 2013 or other statutes not paid.
Undisputed audit fees include expense incurred by Auditor
Exception: In case of sick unit, above prohibition of acceptance shall not apply.
Sick unit means unit regd. for >= 5 years & has accumulated loss >= Net worth.
Mr. C accepted statutory audit of M/s PSU Ltd, whose net worth is negative for year 2022-23.
Audit was to be conducted for year 2023-24. Audited accounts for year 2023-24 showed liabilit
payment of tax audit fees of  15,000 in favour of Mr. E, previous auditor.
(Mention Ch-VII & Exception of Sick unit)
Conclusion: Mr. C not guilty of professional misconduct since M/s PSU Ltd. is a sick unit having neg
net worth for the year 2022-23

CAIP shall not hold at any time appointment of more than "specified no. of audit assignments
