Difference between Division II (Ind AS- Other than NBFC) &
Division III (Ind AS NBFC) of Schedule III
INP
N-B
1. NBFC allowed to present items of BS in order of liquidity.
2. NBFC to disclose by way of note item of 'other income/expense' > 1% of total income. Division II requires
disclosure of item > 1% of Revenue or 10 L (whichever is higher)
3. NBFC separately disclose under receivables, debt due from LLP where Director is partner/member
4. Disclose items comprising 'Revenue from operations' & 'OCI' on face of P&L instead of only notes.
5. Separate disclosure of Trade Receivables with significant increase in cr risk & cr impaired.
6. Conditions or restrictions for distribution attached to statutory reserves separately disclosed.
Sec-Prem 
Note: NBFCs required to comply with Ind AS as per Rule 4 (1)(iv) of Companies (Ind AS) Rules, 2015 &
as amended by Companies (Ind AS) (Amendment) Rules, 2023.
CARO 2020 Reporting
Clause (iii) Para 3 CARO 2020 except sub clause (a) & (e) + Clause (xvi) Para 3 CARO 2020
Eg. AF Ltd. is NBFC & accepting public deposits & giving loans since 2015. Having net owned funds of ₹1.5
Cr & not having registration certificate from RBI & applied on 30.03.25.
Advise auditor w.r.t auditor procedures & reporting requirements of CARO 2020?
As per Clause (xvi) of Para 3 of CARO 2020, report that "whether Co. is required to be regd. u/s 45-IA
of RBI Act, 1934 & if so, whether registration obtained." Examine whether Co. is engaged in business
which requires registration. Registration is required where financing activity is a principal business of
Co. RBI restrict Cos. from carrying on business of NBFI without certificate of registration.
Audit Procedures and Reporting:
Examine transactions of Co. w.r.t activities covered under RBI Act & directions.
FS should be examined to ascertain whether Co's financial assets > 50% of total assets & income from
financial assets > 50% of gross income.
Whether Co. has net owned funds as required for registration as NBFC.
Whether Co. has obtained registration as NBFC, if not, reasons should be sought & documented.
Auditor report incorporating following:-
a. Whether registration is required u/s 45-IA of the RBI Act, 1934.
b. If so, whether it has obtained registration.
c. If registration not obtained, reasons thereof.
Co. was having net owned funds of  1.5 Cr which is less than prescribed limit & not having RBI registration
(though applied for it on 30.03.25). Report as per Clause (xvi) of Para 3 of CARO 2020.

F
In India, Govt. audit is performed by C&AG through Indian Audit & Accounts Deptt.
Articles of Constitution
MCOD Article 148
• Appointment of C&AG by President
 Spl. procedure for removal of C&AG only on proven misbehavior or incapacity.
• Salary & other conditions of service to be determined by Parliament.
Article 149
C&AG's (Duties, Powers & Conditions of Service) Act, 1971 defines functions & powe
of C&AG.
Article 150
Article 151
ay
On advice of C&AG, President to prescribe form in which a/cs of Union & States be ke
Audit Report of C&AG on a/cs of CG/SG submitted to President/Governor of State
be laid before Parliament/State Legislative Assemblies.
Guy C&AG shall hold office for term of 6 years or upto age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.
☑
Organizations subject to C&AG Audit
All Union & State Govt. deptts & offices including Indian Railways & Posts & Telecom.
✓ Public commercial enterprises controlled by Union & State govts, i.e. govt Cos. & corporations.
☑
☑
✓ Non-commercial autonomous bodies & authorities owned or controlled by Union/States.
Authorities & bodies substantially financed from Union/State revenues.
☑
Dept & Offices >> Commercial + Non Commercial >> Substantially financed
Audit of Govt Cos. [Govt holds equity >= 51%]
1. Power to appoint Government Co. Auditor :-
C&AG shall appoint auditor & direct such auditor manner in which a/cs to be audited & auditor
submit a copy of $A/R$ to C&AG which include,
✓ directions, if any, issued by C&AG,
action taken thereon &
its impact on a/cs & FS of Co.
He has right to conduct a supplementary audit & test audit if necessary.
Audit of $a/c$ by C&AG  Submission of a/cs & reports  3 committees
[Constituted by Parliament & State Legislature]
3 Committees that Consider Audit Reports of C&AG
blic Accounts Committee (PAC): Duty to satisfy that:
at moneys were disbursed legally for service/purpose for which they were applied
 expenditure incurred was authorised
re-appropriation made as per provisions (i.e.distribution of funds).

3. Committee on Public Undertakings (COPU)
COPU exercises same financial control on PSUs as PAC exercises over Govt depts.
✓ to examine reports & accounts of PSUs
☑
☑
to examine reports of C&AG on public undertakings
to examine autonomy & efficiency of PSU & whether they are managed as per sound business principles
& prudent commercial practices
✓ other functions of PAC & Estimates Committee not covered above & may be allotted by Speaker.
Parliament
Examine a/cs + C&AG report >> Autonmy & efficiency (SBP + PCP) >> Other functions
C&AGs Role as Friend, Philosopher & Guide to committees
- His report forms basis of committee's working, they can also examining other issues.
Scrutinises notes submitted by ministries to committees to check correctness of submissions
Financial Committees present Report to Parliament/ State Legislature with their observations
recommendations.
Ministries / Govt Dept. to inform Committees of action taken by them on recommendations accepted o
Committees & Committees present Action Taken Reports to Parliament / Legislature.
If $A/R$ couldn't be discussed in detail, written ans. obtained from dept/ministries to ensure A/R no
taken lightly by Govt even if entire report not discussed by committee..
Objective & Scope of PSU Audit
Audit of PSU not limited to Financial & Compliance audit: Also covers performance audit.
Propriety audit: Examination of mgt decisions in sales, purchases etc. to see whether taken in the bes
interests of entity.
Comprehensive audit: Conduct appraisal or efficiency-cum performance audit
Organisations decisions to be taken up by competent authority
Helping govt: Bring out financial & operational deficiencies etc. & analyse causes.
Highlight issues of efficient & economic operations
Fiscal Accountability: Audit of provision of funds, sanctions, compliances & propriety
Managerial Accountability: Efficiency, Economy & effectiveness (Performance audit)

Principles of PSU Audit
General Principles: Ethics & Independence, Professional Judgement, due care & skepticism, Qual
Control, Audit Team mgt & Skill, Audit Risk, Materiality, Documentation, Communication
Principles relating to Audit process
Planning the audit (Agreeing terms of engg, understanding entity, developing audit plan)
Conducting the Audit (Performing audit procedures, evaluating evidence & drawing conclusions)
Reporting & follow up (Reporting based on conclusions & following up on reported matters)

Financial Audit is conducted to express audit opinion on on FS FS & enhance confidence of intended use
C&AG shall express opinion as to whether FS are prepared, in all material respects, as per AFRF.
Compliance Audit
Independent assessment of whether given subject matter is in compliance with applicable criteria
Assessing whether activities, financial transactions & info. comply with regulatory & other rules.
Compliance audit is concerned with:
egularity-adherence of subject matter to relevant laws, regulations & agreements applicable to
opriety- observe general principles of sound financial mgt & ethical conduct of public officials.

Compliance Audit Process
1. General principles & Annual Compliance Audit Plan
Same
Consider principles with ethical significance & relating to Compliance audit. Determine Auditable entitie
Develop annual plan
2. Planning Compliance Audits
Determine objective & scope, audit strategy & plan, Understand entity & its environment, IC, materiality
Assess risk, Plan audit procedures
Performing Audit & Gathering Evidence
Gather evidence, Continually update planning & risk assessment, Consider non-compliance of laws.
Evaluating Evidence & Forming Conclusions
Evaluate whether SAAE obtained, Consider materiality for reporting, form conclusions, documentatio
communication & quality control.
Reporting: Prepare report, responses from entity, follow-up previous reports.
Performance Audit
Objective & systematic examination of evidence
☑ for independent assessment of performance of govt organization, program, etc to provide info to
✓ improve public accountability &
✓ facilitate decision making by parties responsible to oversee or initiate corrective action.
onducted by? C&AG through subordinate offices of IAAD.
ubordinate offices are guided by manual & auditing standards prescribed by C&AG.
Eco-Mode]
onomy: Minimize cost of resources used for an activity, having regard to qty, quality andbest price.
ficiency: Input-output ratio. Max output with min input. or min input for given quality & qty of outp
eck:
Sound procurement policies followed
Resources are properly protected & maintained
Efficient utilization of physical, financial & HR
Public sector prog, entities etc. efficiently managed
Efficient operating procedures used

Compliance Audit Process Esame Same as Normal andit andit,
1. General principles & Annual Compliance Audit Plan
Consider principles with ethical significance & relating to Compliance audit. Determine Auditable entities
Develop annual plan
2. Planning Compliance Audits
Determine objective & scope, audit strategy & plan, Understand entity & its environment, IC, materiality,
Assess risk, Plan audit procedures
3. Performing Audit & Gathering Evidence
Gather evidence, Continually update planning & risk assessment, Consider non-compliance of laws.
4. Evaluating Evidence & Forming Conclusions
Evaluate whether SAAE obtained, Consider materiality for reporting, form conclusions, documentation,
communication & quality control.
5. Reporting: Prepare report, responses from entity, follow-up previous reports.

Determining audit criteria: Standards to determine if program meets expectations.

Deciding audit approach: Determining methods & means to conduct performance audit.
07 PSU Audi
 Mat 25)
Methods & means for conducting Performance audit
Analysis of procedures: Review of systems for planning, conducting, checking & monitoring the activity-
Examination of docs such as financial reports, budgets, guidelines, procedure manuals, etc.
Case studies: Descriptive analysis of entity, scheme etc.
Use of existing data: Investigate data held by entity mgt & other sources.
Surveys: Collecting info. from population to assess interrelation of events & conditions.
Analysis of results: Carry out actual output-input analysis to determine efficiency of programme.
Quantitative analysis: Examination of available financial data like earnings, revenue, or data relating t
programme implementation like details of beneficiaries etc. May not be possible to work with complet
data. In such cases, sampling techniques can be used.

Propriety Audit
Bring out cases of improper, avoidable, or infructuous expenditure even though incurred as per
rules & regulations.
Building may be constructed for installing a telephone exchange but not used for the same
resulting in infructuous expenditure or school building may be constructed but used after 5 year
completion is a case of avoidable expenditure.
Paisa Block
Principles of Propriety
Expenditure not prima facie more than what occasion demands & official exercises same dec
vigilance as person of ordinary prudence would do with his own money
Authority utilising power to sanction expenditure not accruing to its own benefit
Funds not to be utilised to benefit particular person(s)
Apart from agreed remuneration & reward no other indirect benefit to mgt or employees
Functions of Auditor in context of Propriety Audit:
see that expenditure incurred are properly planned.
(Nov 23  ) sm
see that size of expenditure are rightful & expected to give maximum results.
appraise whether those expenditure are likely to give optimum result.
see that any substitute plan of action can bring improvement & return from capital expenditure.
examine that actions & decisions of mgt conductive to public interests & meet standards of cond

Results of comprehensive appraisals of selected undertakings conducted by Audit board
details
esume of Co. Auditor's reports submitted under directions of C&AG & comments on $\overline{a/cs}$ of Gov
ignificant results of audit of undertakings not taken up for appraisal by audit board.

Indicative Objectives & scope of Internal Audit
Monitoring of ICs: Performing 3 way matching of PO, receipt of material (GRN) & vendor invoices bef
approving vendor payment
Examination of financial & operating info.: Internal Audit of sales records, delivery records, se
Commission for identifying correctness of revenue recorded
Review of operating activities: Reviewing Inventory mgt & proper handling to prevent damages
Review of compliance with laws & regulations: Compliance with newly applicable Tax Regime.
Risk management: Evaluation & mgt of Risk Exposure for complex financial instruments
Governance: Assessment of Governance Process in achieving objectives on ethics & values.
Applicability of Internal Audit
Sec 138 of Cos. Act, 2013 read with Rule 13 of Cos. (Accounts) Rules 2014
Listed Cos
Unlisted public Cos.

08a Internal Au
AB Pvt. Ltd. Co, having o/s loans & borrowings from banks > ₹100 Cr, wants to appoint Mr. X, a practic
cost accountant, as an internal auditor. Is appointment of Mr. X valid?
Appointment of Mr. X as internal auditor of AB Pvt. Ltd is valid.
Critical Activities performed by Internal Audit Function
Define overall plan, scope & methodology of Internal Audit Function on a periodic basis.
Oversee & monitor various audit assignments (their proper planning, execution, reporting of finding
subsequent closure of reported observations).
Plan, acquire, engage & review performance, training & development of professional staff, talent & of
resources.
Identify, source, engage & manage external experts & technical solutions, if required.
Communicate & engage with all key stakeholders regarding progress & achievement of objectives.
Develop & maintain a quality evaluation & improvement program..

Review of 3-way matching IC involves matching of PO, GRN & Invoice to ensure all ordered quantity
of intended goods received & invoiced. Failure of this IC may involve over-invoicing, overpayment,
non-receipt, or under-receipt of goods.
✓ Controls should be inbuilt in operating functions to make it cost effective
Separate credit control department not justified if objective of reducing credit risk & minimising
debt recovery period can be met through controls in-built in $a/c$ & sales systems, for smaller &
medium-sized concerns,
✓ Consider limitations of IC, cost benefit, human errors, collusion & abuse by process owner

intention, etc...

Integrity, Objectivity & Independence of Internal Auditor
1. Be free from any undue influences. Independence not only in mind but also in appearance.
Resist any undue pressure in his assignments. Organisation structure of key personnel, positio
reporting of Chief Internal Auditor, powers & authority derived from superiors establis
independence.
2. Be honest, truthful & person of high integrity.
3. Conduct work in highly objective manner. Not allow bias, in conclusions or reporting his opinion.
Eg. (i) to avoid conflict of interest, not review activity for which he was previously responsible.

(ii) for listed Co., internal auditor may report directly to TCWG, such as Audit Committee instead
CEO/CFO. Internal auditor should immediately bring any conflict of interest to mgt's attention so
corrective action may be taken.

08a Internal Audit
Performing Internal Audit Engg.
Learn
With
Fun
Step 1- Obtain knowledge of Business & its Environment
Conduct meetings with key stakeholders to understand business environment & top mgt's expectations.
Understand various business documents - SOPs & F.S Etc.
Also understand IT landscape, various applications & ERP systems & MIS of organization.
Step 2 - Perform Audit Planning
Audit scope approved by Audit Committee & BOD.
Once approved, share detailed Audit Plan with KMPs.
Meeting with key stakeholders before start of audit & share details of Info. & System Access required.
Detailed work plan prepared by audit managers & approved by Head of IA / Chief Internal Auditor.
Plan prepared after evaluating major risks & checks to assess control environment to mitigate such risks.
♡
Step 3 - Gather required info.
Obtain regd info. & checks correctness & integrity of info. Obtain info, directly from source.
Adequate planning & advance intimation should be made for any interim info. needed for audit checks.
Step 4 - Perform audit checks
Variation's
Analytical Procedures: To identify key trends & outliers. Relevant analytical tools may be used.
Sampling: Select sample. Detailed audit testing performed as per plan.
Evidence: Adequate evidences collected & stored. Prepare lists of issues & controls gaps.
Reporting: Interim reports may be issued after review of work.
✔Documentation: Adequate document of work papers.
Step 5 - Reporting of Internal Audit Issues
☑ Prepare draft report of Internal Audit including business process reviewed as per scope, detailed audit
coverage & exclusions, period covered, summary with detailed issues over gaps & recommendations.
 Mgt Action Plan should be agreed with responsibility & timelines for actions..
☑ Also review status of actions taken by mgt agreed during previous audits & report in audit report.
✓ Circulate Final Report & present findings to Audit Committee.
SIA 370 Reporting by IA in 2 stages:
2 Stages Reporting
1) At end of assignment, report covering specific area/function of entity is prepared highlighting ke
observations. Details of manner of conducting assignment & key findings from audit. Issued to Audite
with copies to local mgt agreed in planning phase.
2) On periodic basis, at close of plan period, comprehensive report on IA activities covering Entity & pla
period is prepared by Chief Internal Auditor (or EP for external service provider). Normally done a
Quarterly basis & submitted to Audit Committee.
This SIA pertains to responsibility to issue only IA report pertaining to specific assignments.
8a.5

ep 1- Obtain knowledge of Business & its Environment
nduct meetings with key stakeholders to understand business environment & top mgt's expectations.
derstand various business documents - SOPs & F.S Etc.
so understand IT landscape, various applications & ERP systems & MIS of organization.
tep 2 - Perform Audit Planning
udit scope approved by Audit Committee & BOD.
nce approved, share detailed Audit Plan with KMPs.

etailed work plan prepared by audit managers & approved by Head of IA / Chief Internal Auditor.

Step 3 - Gather required info.
Obtain reqd info. & checks correctness & integrity of info. Obtain info. directly from source.
Adequate planning & advance intimation should be made for any interim info. needed for audit checks.
Step 4 - Perform audit checks
Variations
Analytical Procedures: To identify key trends & outliers. Relevant analytical tools may be used.
Sampling: Select sample. Detailed audit testing performed as per plan.
Evidence: Adequate evidences collected & stored. Prepare lists of issues & controls gaps.
Reporting: Interim reports may be issued after review of work.
Documentation: Adequate document of work papers.
Step 5 - Reporting of Internal Audit Issues
Prepare draft report of Internal Audit including business process reviewed as per scope, detailed audi
coverage & exclusions, period covered, summary with detailed issues over gaps & recommendations.
Mgt Action Plan should be agreed with responsibility & timelines for actions..
Also review status of actions taken by mgt agreed during previous audits & report in audit report.
Circulate Final Report & present findings to Audit Committee.
SIA 370 Reporting by IA in 2 stages:
2 Stages Reporting
At end of assignment, report covering specific area/function of entity is prepared highlighting ke
observations. Details of manner of conducting assignment & key findings from audit. Issued to Audite
with copies to local mgt agreed in planning phase.
On periodic basis, at close of plan period, comprehensive report on IA activities covering Entity & ple
period is prepared by Chief Internal Auditor (or EP for external service provider). Normally done
Quarterly basis & submitted to Audit Committee.
This SIA pertains to responsibility to issue only IA report pertaining to specific assignments.

Executive Summary
Summary of critical findings

08a Internal
Rating of highlighted issues $(Eg~High/Medium/Low)$ as per criteria approved by Audit Committee
Audit recommendation to improve control environment & address the highlighted finding

Target timelines for action
Key Elements of Internal Audit Report
a) Overview of scope, objective & approach of audit assignments
b) Fact that Internal audit completed as per Standard on Internal Audit
c) Executive summary of key observations covering all important aspects & specific to scope of assigr
d) Summary of corrective actions required(or agreed by mgt) for each observation
e) Nature of assurance, if any, which can be derived from observations.

Scope & objective of Internal audit dependent on size & structure of entity & requirements of mgt.
IA reviews a/c systems & IC, examines financial & operating info for mgt, there's lot of overlap between
work of IA & EA.
Work done by IA has important bearing on work of EA. IA is integral part of system of IC.
Audit committee in consultation with IA formulate scope, functioning, etc for conducting Internal audit.
• Obligatory for EA to examine scope & effectiveness of IA work.
He should examine internal audit dept, strength of staff & qualification & powers.
. Extent of independence exhibited by IA & status in organisation, determine effectiveness of audit.
EA should evaluate internal audit function to determine NTE of compliance & substantive procedures.

Management
Miscellaneous Topics
Internal Audit as a Mgt Function
It's a process by which goals are attained through optimum utilisation of all resources.
Mgt is a set of 5 general functions: planning, organizing, staffing, directing & controlling.
• While functions of planning, organizing, staffing & leading help to create & grow stakeholder's
while controlling is to preserve stakeholder's wealth.
Internal Audit (IA)
"IA provides independent assurance on effectiveness of ICs & risk mgt processes to enhance gove
& achieve organisational objectives".
Evaluate mgt activities & advise on improving ICs & manage risks by recommending mitigating con-
IA is important element of mgt controlling function
✓ Helps mgt to set up systems & processes to mitigate risk. (New)
✓ Report on identified gaps & areas of weak internal control
 ld Strengthonch)
✓ Identify root cause of problems & suggest mitigating steps & strengthen ICs.
Conclusion: IA important function that helps mgt to achieve org. goals & work in an orderly manne
Audit Trail
Audit Trail (or Edit Log) is visible trail of evidence for tracing info. in reports to original source.
Audit trails are chronological record of changes made to data. Any change i.e. creating, updat
deleting data must be recorded.
Records maintained as audit trail may include following info.:
➤ when changes were made i.e., date & time (timestamp)
 who made the change i.e., User Id
 what data was changed i.e., data/transaction reference; success/failure
To demonstrate that audit trail feature was functional, operated & not disabled, Co. would have to c
7
 & implement specific ICs which would be evaluated by auditors, as appropriate.
☐
☑
☑
☑
☑
☑
Illustrative list of controls to be implemented & operated are given below:
Controls to ensure that:
audit trail feature hasn't been disabled or deactivated
access to audit trail (& backups) is disabled/restricted & access logs are maintained.
changes to configurations of audit trail are authorized & logs of such changes are maintained.
User IDs are assigned to each individual & User IDs are not shared 
periodic backups of audit trails taken & archived as per statutory period.
[Last Back up)

CHAPTER
086
SA 610
SA 610: Using the work of Internal Auditor (IA)
Sole responsibility for audit opinion → External Auditor (EA)
Lea
Fu
EA has to obtain SAAE that work of IA Function or Internal auditor providing direct assistance
adequate for purpose of audit
Internal Audit Function: Fn. of entity that performs assurance & consulting activities to evaluat
improve effectiveness of entity's governance, risk mgt & ICs (GRC).
Direct Assistance: Use of internal auditors to perform audit procedures under direction, supervisio
review (DSR) of external auditor.
Scope of SA:
a) Using work of IA Function in obtaining audit evidence &
b) Using IA to provide direct assistance under direction, supervision and review (DSR) of EA
This SA doesn't apply if entity doesn't have IA function/Internal auditor
Objectives of Auditor
To determine if work of IA function or direct assistance of Internal auditor can be used
• If using work of IA Function, determine whether work is adequate for Audit purpose
• If using Internal Auditor to provide Direct Asst, to DSR their work
Internal Audit Function
oes
Evaluating whether work of Internal Audit Function can be used for Audit Purpose
a. Extent to which IA function's organizational status & relevant P&P support objectivity of IA
b. Level of competence of IA function &
c. Whether IA function applies a systematic & disciplined approach, including quality control.
Determining Nature & Extent of work of Internal Audit Function that can be used
Types of Work of IA function that can be used by external auditor include following:
Testing of operating effectiveness of controls.
☑ Substantive procedures involving limited judgment.
Observations of inventory counts.
N.B
Tracing transactions through the information system relevant to financial reporting.
Testing of compliance with regulatory requirements.
In some circumstances, audits or reviews of financial info. of subsidiaries that aren't signific
components to group (where this does not conflict with requirements of SA 600).

086 SA
External auditor shall make all significant judgments in audit & prevent undue use of work of IA functio
shall plan to use less of work of function & perform more of work directly:
a) More judgment is involved in:
(i) Planning & performing relevant audit procedures &
(ii) Evaluating audit evidence gathered;
b) Higher the assessed RoMM at assertion level, with special consideration given to significant risks
c) Less the IA function's organizational status & relevant P&P adequately support objectivity of IA &
d) Lower the level of competence of IA function.
Notes:
Extent of involvement of external auditor: Be sufficiently involved in audit
Communicate with TCWG how external auditor planned to use work of IA function, while sharing planne
scope & timing of the audit.
Using Work of Internal Audit Function
Discussion & Coordination with IA Function: Discuss the planned use of its work with function fo
coordinating their respective activities.
Read reports of IA function relating to the work that external auditor plans to use to obtai
understanding of nature & extent of audit procedures it performed & related findings.
External auditor shall perform sufficient audit procedures to determine adequacy of IA function fo
purposes of audit, including evaluating whether:
 . Work of IA function been properly planned, performed, supervised, reviewed & documented
D. SAAE obtained to enable the function to draw reasonable conclusions &
 . Conclusions reached are appropriate & reports prepared are consistent with results of work.
ature & extent of external auditor's audit procedures shall be depend on evaluation of:
Amount of judgment involved
Assessed RoMM

b. Evaluation of existence & significance of threats shall include inquiry of internal auditors regardin
interests & relationships that may create a threat to their objectivity.
Factors to be considered while evaluating existence & significance of
SSAFI threats to objectivity of Internal Auditor
Extent to which IA function's organisation status & relevant P&P support objectivity of IA
Family & personal relationships with individual responsible for aspect of entity to which work relates.
Association with division or department in entity to which work relates.
Significant financial interests in entity other than remuneration on terms consistent with thos
applicable to other employees.
External auditor shall not use an internal auditor to provide direct assistance if:
a. There are significant threats to objectivity of internal auditor or
b. Internal auditor lacks sufficient competence to perform proposed work.
c. This amounts to weaknesses in Internal Control System if internal auditor does not apply required tes
procedures considering Materiality. This would result in Higher Audit Risks.
Factors to consider:
Determining Nature & Extent of Work that Can Be Assigned to
Internal Auditors Providing Direct Assistance
1. Amount of judgment involved in:
(i) Planning & performing audit procedures &
(ii) Evaluating audit evidence gathered
2. Assessed RoMM &
Inf
3. External auditor's evaluation of existence & significance of threats to objectivity & level of competenc
of internal auditors providing such assistance.
Written Agreements: Prior to using IA to provide direct assistance for purposes of audit
■. From authorized representative of entity that IA will be allowed to follow external auditor's instructions
& entity will not intervene in work IA performs for external auditor &
. From internal auditors that they will keep confidential specific matters instructed by external audito
and inform about any threat to their objectivity.
Areas where Internal auditor can't provide Direct Assistance
- Involve making significant judgments in audit
-Relate to higher assessed ROMM where judgment required in performing relevant audit procedures a
evaluating audit evidence gathered is more than limited
Work with which internal auditors have been involved & which has already been, or will be, reported t
Mgt or TCWG by IA function or

•
Involves analysis carried out before acquiring a controlling interest in Co. to conform business conditions.
Also, it can apply to recommendation for investment or advancing a loan/credit.
Who can do DD? Any person.
Areas where due diligence may be undertaken are: -
Corporate restructuring, Venture capital financing, Public offerings
Difference between DD & Audit
DD is different from audit. Audit is independent examination of FS of organization to express opinion.
DD refers to examination of potential investment to confirm all material facts of prospective business
opportunity. Involves reviewing financial & nonfinancial records. Policies / Ethics.
DD is the care a reasonable person should take before entering into agreement with another party.
Importance of D.D. [Reasons for DD]
To identify potential 'deal killer' defects in target Co. & avoid bad business transaction
To verify that transaction complies with investment/acquisition criteria.
To confirm that business is what it appears to be
To gain info. useful for valuing assets, defining representations & warranties, &/or negotiating price
concessions.
Read
2
Identify → Verify Confirm → Gain info.
Branches ↓

a. How well is Co. able to turn its trade receivables & inventories?
b. Is Co. able to honor its commitments to trade payables, banks, govt & other stakeholders?
C. How well does it deploy its funds?
d. Are there any funds lying idle or Co. is able to reap maximum benefits out of available funds?

promised, key employees who will not continue.
h) Statutory compliance: If not legally compliant may have to pay penalty.
Work approach to DD
→ Reviewing & reporting on financials submitted by target Co.
→ Assessing business first hand by a site visit (if applicable).
→ Working through DD process with acquisitioning Co. or investor by defining key areas.
→ Helping prepare an offer based on completion of DD.

Forensic Accounting Services
Indicative list of services that can be provided:
FS manipulations
RP transactions/valuations (ALP)
Anti-Money laundering
Suspicious transactions under IBC
Valuations/Estimations of loss/damage, Licence Fees/Dues/Tax Evasion,
Fund diversions/Asset tracing
Forensic professional is often involved in:
1. Fraud Detection: Investigating & analyzing financial evidence, detecting financial frauds & tracir
misappropriated funds.,
2. Computer Forensics: Developing computerized applications to assist in recovery, analysis & presentatio
of financial evidence.  Emails/poc s .
3. Fraud Prevention: Either reviewing ICs to verify adequacy or consultation in development
implementation of ICs as per organization's risk profile.

1. Fraud Detection: Investigating & analyzing financial evidence, detecting financial frauds & tracing
misappropriated funds,,
2. Computer Forensics: Developing computerized applications to assist in recovery, analysis & presentation
of financial evidence. Emails/Docls]
3. Fraud Prevention: Either reviewing ICs to verify adequacy or consultation in development &
implementation of ICs as per organization's risk profile.
4. Providing Expert Testimony: Assisting in legal proceedings, including testifying in court as expert witness
and preparing visual aids
Investigang  and be skiled in collecting evidence to be used in a court case within stipulated
time & keeping a clear chain of custody until evidence is presented in court.
✓ If evidence is inconclusive or there are gaps in chain of custody, evidence may be challenged in court
or become inadmissible.
✓ Be alert to documents being falsified, damaged or destroyed by the suspect(s).
4. Perform the analysis:
Actual analysis performed will depend upon nature of assignment & may involve:
✓ calculating economic damages
-19 performing present value calculations utilizing appt. discount rates
summarizing a large no. of transactions
✓ utilizing charts & graphics to explain the analysis
utilizing a computerized application like a spread sheet, data base or computer model
✓ performing a regression/sensitivity analysis
Reporting
✓ Final step of forensic accounting. Include info. about fraudulent activity, if any found.
✓ Report findings, evidence, conclusion of amount of loss & identify those involved in fraud.
✓ May include sections on nature of assignment, scope, approach, limitations & findings &/or opinions.
✓ Include schedules & graphics to support & explain findings.
✓ Discuss how fraudster set up fraud scheme & controls, if any, were circumvented.
✓ Recommend improvements to controls to prevent similar frauds in future.
✓ Report should be based on facts & not on opinion of person writing report.

May include sections on nature of assignment, scope, approach, limitations & findings &/or opinions.

✓ Scope & objectives of assignment
✓ Approach & broad work procedures undertaken
✓ Executive Summary of results, covering all important aspects & essence of findings
✓ Reference to use of expert, where applicable foregisic Alcling & investigation standards
✓ Fact that assignment conducted as per FAIS or any material departures there from
✓ List of findings supported by key evidences, sources of evidences, & other relevant matter
✓ Assumptions, limitations & disclaimers of assignment
✓ Conclusions (if any) drawn from assessment.

4. Discussion of Draft report: Where engg. requires discussion of findings with subject party (chor) prior
to finalisation, summary of responses recd. from them shall be included in report.
→ Further, Principles of Natural Justice requires discussion of observations with subject party.
→ Sometimes, done by Primary Stakeholders through internal processes Eg disciplinary committee etc.
→ At times, Professional is requested to discuss draft findings as part of interview process with subject.
5. Assumptions & Limitations: List relevant assumptions made during assignment.
→ In addition, Professional may encounter limitations that restrict procedures applied.
 Such limitations include lack of mgt support, restricted (or denied) access to records, info, or people.
 These disclaimers would be covered as key element of report.
Report shall not express an opinion or pass any judgement on guilt or

3. Format or Content of Report: No fixed format, report shall include certain key elements to enabl
recipient to understand purpose of assignment, scope of work, limitations, assumptions or disclaimers
facts, & evidence, & conclusions. Where form & content of report is mandated by stakeholders, report as
per those requirements including key elements.
Key Elements of Report
✓ Title, addressee & distribution list (if any)
✓ Scope & objectives of assignment
☑ Approach & broad work procedures undertaken
☑ Executive Summary of results, covering all important aspects & essence of findings
☑ Reference to use of expert, where applicable
→ Forergisic Alcling & investigation standards
✓ Fact that assignment conducted as per FAIS or any material departures there from
✓ List of findings supported by key evidences, sources of evidences, & other relevant matter
✓ Assumptions, limitations & disclaimers of assignment
✓ Conclusions (if any) drawn from assessment.

SA 800 Series

SA 800, Special Considerations - Audits of FS prepared as per Special Purpose Frameworks (SPF)
SPF is a FRF designed to meet financial info. needs of specific users.
May be fair presentation or compliance framework.
Examples of Special purpose frameworks (SPFs): -
Cash basis of accounting & cash flow info. that entity may be required to prepare for creditors.
FR provisions established by regulator to meet requirements of regulator.
FR provisions of contract, eg. bond indenture, loan agreement, or project grant.
FRF to meet common financial info. needs of wide range of users → "General purpose framework"
FRF to meet financial info. needs of specific users → "Special purpose framework"
Note: Reference to "FS" in this SA means "complete set of SPFS" including related notes.
Related notes include summary of significant a/c policies & other explanatory info.
Considerations when Accepting such assignment
Main focus Determining Acceptability of FRF
For SPFS, financial info. needs of intended users are key factor in determining acceptability of FRF.
Applicable FRF
FR Stds established by
authorised or recognised org.
Some L/R may prescribe FRF for SPFS for certain
types of entity. Eg Requirements of Regulator.
Note: Where FR standards supplemented by legislative or regulatory requirements. SA 210 requir
auditor to determine whether any conflicts between FR standards & additional requirements exist
prescribes actions if such conflicts exist.
AFRF may include FR provisions of contract or other sources.
Acceptability of FRF determined by considering attributes of acceptable FRFs as per SA 210,
For eg, for valuing net assets on date of sale, vendor & purchaser may agree on very prudent estime
of allowances for uncollectible $a/c$ receivable, even though such financial info. isn't neutral compe
with financial info. prepared as per a general purpose framework.
Considerations When Planning & Performing Such Audit
SA 200 requires auditor to comply with
a, relevant ethical requirements, including independence & b. all SAs relevant to audit.
Comply with each requirement of SA unless entire SA or requirement isn't relevant.
In exceptional circumstances, may depart from SA by performing alternative audit procedures.

SA 800, Special Considerations - Audits of FS prepared as per Special Purpose Frameworks (SPF)
SPF is a FRF designed to meet financial info. needs of specific users.
May be fair presentation or compliance framework.
Examples of Special purpose frameworks (SPFs): -
Cash basis of accounting & cash flow info. that entity may be required to prepare for creditors.
FR provisions established by regulator to meet requirements of regulator.
FR provisions of contract, eg. bond indenture, loan agreement, or project grant.
✓ FRF to meet common financial info. needs of wide range of users → "General purpose framework"
✓ FRF to meet financial info. needs of specific users → "Special purpose framework"
Note: Reference to "FS" in this SA means "complete set of SPFS" including related notes.
Related notes include summary of significant a/c policies & other explanatory info.
Considerations when Accepting such assignment
Main focus → Determining Acceptability of FRF
For SPFS, financial info. needs of intended users are key factor in determining acceptability of FRF.
Applicable FRF
FR Stds established by
authorised or recognised org.
Some L/R may prescribe FRF for SPFS for certain
types of entity. Eg Requirements of Regulator.
Note: Where FR standards supplemented by legislative or regulatory requirements. SA 210 requires
auditor to determine whether any conflicts between FR standards & additional requirements exist &
prescribes actions if such conflicts exist.
AFRF may include FR provisions of contract or other sources.
Acceptability of FRF determined by considering attributes of acceptable FRFs as per SA 210.
For eg, for valuing net assets on date of sale, vendor & purchaser may agree on very prudent estimates
of allowances for uncollectible $a/c$ receivable, even though such financial info. isn't neutral compared
with financial info. prepared as per a general purpose framework.
Considerations When Planning & Performing Such Audit
SA 200 requires auditor to comply with
a. relevant ethical requirements, including independence & b. all SAs relevant to audit.
Comply with each requirement of SA unless entire SA or requirement isn't relevant.
In exceptional circumstances, may depart from SA by performing alternative audit procedures.
10.1
Learn
With
Fun
10 SA 800 Series
Application of some SAs in audit of SPFS may require special consideration by auditor (Examples)
Materiality [SA 320]
Judgments about matters material based on consideration of common financial info. needs of users.
In audit of SPFS, those judgments based on consideration of financial info. needs of intended users.
SA 315 requires auditor to obtain understanding of entity's selection & application of a/c policies.
In case of FS prepared as per provisions of a contract, obtain understanding of any significant
interpretations of contract that mgt made in preparation of FS.
An interpretation is significant when adoption of another reasonable interpretation would have produced
a material difference in info. presented in FS.
For SPFS, prepared as per contract, mgt may agree with intended users on a threshold below which
misstatements identified will not be corrected or adjusted.
Existence of such threshold doesn't relieve auditor from determining materiality as per SA 320 for
planning & performing audit of SPFS.
Communication with TCWG [SA 260]
SA 260 requires auditor to determine appropriate person(s) within entity's governance structure to
communicate. In some cases, all of TCWG are involved in managing entity & application of communication
requirements is modified to recognize this position.
When complete set of GPFS is also prepared by entity, those person(s) responsible for oversight of
preparation of SPFS may not be same as TCWG responsible for GPFS.
SA 700 Forming opinion and reporting considerations in such audit
When forming opinion & reporting on SPFS, apply requirements in SA 700.
Red
Description of AFRF
SA 700 requires auditor to evaluate whether FS adequately describe AFRF
(GPFS: General Purpose FS)
If FS prepared as per contract, evaluate whether FS describe significant interpretations of contract.
SA 700 deals with form & content of $A/R$, including specific ordering of certain elements.
For audit report on SPFS: -
Audit report also describe purpose for which FS prepared & if necessary, intended users, or refer to
note in SPFS that contain that info. &
If mat has choice of FRFs, explanation of mgt's responsibility for FS also refer to responsibility for
determining AFRF acceptable in circumstances.
When KAMS communicated in auditor's report on SPFS, SA 701 applies in its entirety.
SA 720: Reports containing SPFS, where purpose is to provide owners (or similar stakeholders) with info.
on SPFS, considered annual reports as per SA 720.
"Similar stakeholders" includes specific users whose financial info, needs are met by SPF used to prepare
SPFS. If entity plans to issue such report, SA 720 applies.

e. As per SA 706, auditor may refer, in OM para in audit report on SPFS, to auditor's report on complete
set of GPFS or to matter(s) reported. For eg, may refer to MURG section included in $A/R$ on GPFS.
 Alerting Readers that FS Are Prepared as per a Special Purpose Framework (SPF)
SPFS may be used for other purposes. Eg. regulator may require entities to place SPFS on public record
To avoid misunderstandings, auditor alerts users of auditor's report using EOM para explaining that
☑ FS are prepared as per SPF &
✓ therefore, may not be suitable for another purpose.
SA 706 requires this para to be included within a separate section of audit report with appropriate
heading that includes the term "Emphasis of Matter".
Example of Emphasis of Matter - Basis of Accounting & Restriction on Distribution & Use
We draw attention to Note X to FS, which describes basis of accounting.
FS are prepared to assist Co. in complying with FR provisions of contract. As a result, FS may not be
suitable for another purpose. Our report is intended solely for Co. & DEF Co. Ltd & should not be
distributed or used by parties other than Co. or DEF Co. Ltd. Our opinion is not modified for this matter.
Restriction on Distribution or Use
In addition to alert, auditor may indicate that $A/R$ is intended solely for specific users.
Depending on $L/R$, this may be achieved by restricting distribution or use of $A/R$.
In these circumstances, para, alerting readers may be expanded to include these other matters &
heading modified accordingly.
SA 805 Spl. Considerations
Audits of Single FS (SFS) & Specific Elements, Accounts or Items of a FS (SEFS)
FS is to be distinguished from complete set of FS. For eg, cash flow statement is single FS.
EFS means an "element, $a/c$ or item of a FS." Eg trade receivables or cash & bank balances.
FS or SEFS includes related notes comprising summary of significant $a/c$ policies & other explanatory
Fo. relevant to FS or element.
amples of Specific Elements, Accounts or Items of a F.S. are:
Counts receivable, allowance for doubtful $a/c$ receivable, inventory, liability for accrued benefits of
pension plan, recorded value of identified intangible assets, or liability for "incurred but not
orted" claims in insurance portfolio, including related notes.
edule of externally managed assets & income of a pvt pension plan, including related notes.
edule of net tangible assets, including related notes.
edule of disbursements in relation to a lease property, including explanatory notes.
edule of profit participation or employee bonuses, including explanatory notes.

Example of Emphasis of Matter - Basis of Accounting & Restriction on Distribution & Use
We draw attention to Note X to FS, which describes basis of accounting.
FS are prepared to assist Co. in complying with FR provisions of contract. As a result, FS may not be

Restriction on Distribution or Use
 
In addition to alert, auditor may indicate that $A/R$ is intended solely for specific users.
 Depending on $L/R$, this may be achieved by restricting distribution or use of $A/R$.
。
In these circumstances, para. alerting readers may be expanded to include these other matters &

SA 805 Spl. Considerations
Audits of Single FS (SFS) & Specific Elements, Accounts or Items of a FS (SEFS)
SFS is to be distinguished from complete set of FS. For eg, cash flow statement is single FS.
SEFS means an "element, $a/c$ or item of a FS." Eg trade receivables or cash & bank balances.
SFS or SEFS includes related notes comprising summary of significant $a/c$ policies & other explanatory
info. relevant to FS or element.
Examples of Specific Elements, Accounts or Items of a F.S. are:
✓ Accounts receivable, allowance for doubtful $a/c$ receivable, inventory, liability for accrued benefits of
pvt pension plan, recorded value of identified intangible assets, or liability for "incurred but not
reported" claims in insurance portfolio, including related notes.
✓ Schedule of externally managed assets & income of a pvt pension plan, including related notes.
✓ Schedule of net tangible assets, including related notes.
 Schedule of disbursements in relation to a lease property, including explanatory notes.
 Schedule of profit participation or employee bonuses, including explanatory notes.

10 SA 800 Series
Note: Maybe prepared as per General/Special Purpose Framerwork. If SPF then SA 800 also applies.
✓ SA 805 doesn't apply to report of component auditor, issued as a result of work performed on financial
info. of component at request of principal auditor for audit of Consol. FS.
Application of SAS
SA 200 requires auditor to comply with all relevant SAs.
In case of audit of SFS or SEFS this requirement applies irrespective of whether auditor also engaged
to audit entity's complete set of FS.
If auditor not auditing complete set FS → determine whether such audit as per SAs is practicable.
SA 200 requires auditor to comply with
(a) relevant ethical requirements, including independence & (b) all SAs relevant to audit.
Comply with each requirement of SA unless entire SA isn't relevant or requirement is not relevant.
In exceptional circumstances, may depart from SA by performing alternative audit procedures.
Compliance with SAs relevant to audit of a SFS or SEFS may not be practicable when auditor not engaged
to audit entity's complete set of FS
 Auditor doesn't have same understanding of entity & its environment, including IC, as auditor who also
audits entity's complete set of FS.
 Also doesn't have audit evidence about general quality of $a/c$ records or other $a/c$ info. that would be
acquired in audit of complete set of FS.
 So, may need further evidence to corroborate audit evidence acquired from $a/c$ records.
 For audit of SEFS, certain SAs require disproportionate audit work. Eg. SA 570 requirements for audit
of
$a/c$ receivable, complying with them may not be practicable because of audit effort required.
 If auditor concludes that audit as per SAs may not be practicable, discuss with mgt whether another
type of engg. might be more practicable.
Read
Acceptability of FRF
SA 210 requires determining acceptability of FRF applied in preparation of FS.
For audit of SFS or SEFS, consider whether application of FRF will result in adequate disclosures
enabling intended users to understand info in FS or element & effect of material transactions & events
on info conveyed in FS or element.
Note:
SFS/SEFS may be prepared as per FRF established by recognised std. setting org. for preparation of
complete set of FS.
Determine acceptability of applicable framework considering whether framework includes all relevant
requirements of framework for presentation of SFS or SEFS that provides adequate disclosures.
Considerations When planning & performing Audit auf,
Auditor shall adapt all SAs relevant to audit as necessary in circumstances of engg.
Even for audit of SEFS, SAs such as SA 240, SA 550 & SA 570 are relevant.

Acceptability of FRF
SA 210 requires determining acceptability of FRF applied in preparation of FS.
For audit of SFS or SEFS, consider whether application of FRF will result in adequate disclosure
enabling intended users to understand info in FS or element & effect of material transactions & event
on info conveyed in FS or element.
Note:
SFS/SEFS may be prepared as per FRF established by recognised std. setting org. for preparation o
complete set of FS..
Determine acceptability of applicable framework considering whether framework includes all relevan
requirements of framework for presentation of SFS or SEFS that provides adequate disclosures.
Considerations When planning & performing Audit Farf
Auditor shall adapt all SAs relevant to audit as necessary in circumstances of engg.
Even for audit of SEFS, SAs such as SA 240, SA 550 & SA 570 are relevant.

Audit Evidence
dlong
When auditing (both) SFS or SEFS, with complete set of FS, may use audit evidence obtained as part of
audit of entity's complete set of FS.
SAs require auditor to obtain SAAE to base opinion on SFS or SEFS.
Inter-relation
Individual FS that includes complete set of FS & elements of those FS, including notes, are interrelated
Hence when auditing SFS or SEFS, may not be able to consider FS or element in isolation.
May need to perform procedures in relation to interrelated items to meet objective of audit.
Materiality
Materiality determined for a SFS or SEFs may be lower than materiality for entity's complete set of FS
Affecting NTE of audit procedures & evaluation of uncorrected misstatements..
Form of Opinion
SA 210 requires agreed terms of audit engg. to include expected form of reports to be issued.
For audit of SFS or SEFS → consider whether expected form of opinion is appropriate.
Applicable FRF?
For SFS or SEFS, AFRF may not explicitly address presentation of FS or element.
This may be case when AFRF is FRF is established for complete set of FS
Consider whether expected form of opinion appropriate in light of AFRF.
Factors that may affect auditor's consideration as to whether to use phrases "presents fairly, in al
material respects", or "gives a true and fair view" in auditor's opinion include:
Whether AFRF is restricted to preparation of complete set of FS.
Whether SFS or SEFS will:
✓ Comply fully with framework relevant to FS or element & presentation includes notes & disclosures
✓ If necessary to achieve fair presentation,

10 SA 800 Ser
 
 provide disclosures beyond those specifically required by framework or
 in exceptional circumstances, depart from a requirement of framework.
Auditor's decision as to expected form of opinion is a matter of professional judgment.
Forming an Opinion & Reporting Considerations
When forming opinion & reporting on SFS or SEFS → apply SA 700
SA 700 requires, in forming opinion, evaluate whether FS provide adequate disclosures to enab
intended users to understand effect of material transactions & events on info, conveyed in FS.
Going Concern: Depending on AFRF used in preparation of SFS or SEFS, description of mgt & audit
responsibilities in audit report relating to going concern may need to be adapted, as necessary.
 Key Audit Matters as per SA 701 for audits of complete sets of general purpose FS of listed entities
Other Info: As per SA 720 reports containing or accompanying SFS or SEFS, where purpose is to provi
owners (or similar stakeholders) with info. on SFS or SEFS, considered to be annual reports.
 
When entity plans to issue such report, SA 720 apply to audit of SFS or SEFS.
Signature: Signed by auditor (i.e. engg. partner) in his personal name & firm name.
Also mention membership no. & firm registration no.
Special Points: [Audit report: $A/R]$
1. Separate Opinion: If $A/R$ on SFS or SEFS with audit of entity's complete set of FS, express separa
opinion for each engg.
2. Published Together: Audited SFS or SEFS may be published together with entity's audited complete s
of FS.
☑
Differentiation: Mos
✓ If auditor concludes presentation of SFS or SEFS doesn't differentiate it from complete set of FS
☐ ask mgt to rectify.
✓ Also differentiate opinion on SFS or SEFS from opinion on complete set of FS.
☑
☑
✓ Not issue $A/R$ containing opinion on SFS or SEFS until satisfied with differentiation.
3. Modified opinion/EOM/OM Para/MURG Section/Uncorrected MM of Other Info.
If $A/R$ on complete set of FS includes modified opinion or EOM/OM para or MURG section
Uncorrected MM of Other Info., consider implications of this on $A/R$ on SFS or SEFS.
Implications of matters included in audit report on complete set of FS for audit of SFS or SEFS
Consider implications of certain matters included in $A/R$ on complete set of FS relevant for audit of S
or SEFS & it's $A/R$. This involves professional judgment. P.
 Factors that may be relevant in considering those implications include:
. Nature of matter(s) described in $A/R$ on complete set of FS & extent to which it relates to SFS/SE

10 SA 800 Serie
Auditor's decision as to expected form of opinion is a matter of professional judgment.
Forming an Opinion & Reporting Considerations
When forming opinion & reporting on SFS or SEFS → apply SA 700
SA 700 requires, in forming opinion, evaluate whether FS provide adequate disclosures to enable
intended users to understand effect of material transactions & events on info. conveyed in FS.
Going Concern: Depending on AFRF used in preparation of SFS or SEFS, description of mgt & auditor
responsibilities in audit report relating to going concern may need to be adapted, as necessary.
Key Audit Matters as per SA 701 for audits of complete sets of general purpose FS of listed entities.
Other Info: As per SA 720 reports containing or accompanying SFS or SEFS, where purpose is to provide
owners (or similar stakeholders) with info. on SFS or SEFS, considered to be annual reports.
When entity plans to issue such report, SA 720 apply to audit of SFS or SEFS.
Signature: Signed by auditor (i.e. engg. partner) in his personal name & firm name.
Also mention membership no. & firm registration no.
Special Points: [Audit report: $A/R]$
Separate Opinion: If $A/R$ on SFS or SEFS with audit of entity's complete set of FS, express separate
opinion for each engg.
- Published Together: Audited SFS or SEFS may be published together with entity's audited complete set
of FS.
Differentiation: Col
If auditor concludes presentation of SFS or SEFS doesn't differentiate it from complete set of FS →
ask mgt to rectify.
Also differentiate opinion on SFS or SEFS from opinion on complete set of FS.
Not issue A/R containing opinion on SFS or SEFS until satisfied with differentiation.
Modified opinion/EOM/OM Para/MURG Section/Uncorrected MM of Other Info.
If $A/R$ on complete set of FS includes modified opinion or EOM/OM para or MURG section on
Uncorrected MM of Other Info., consider implications of this on $A/R$ on SFS or SEFS.
Implications of matters included in audit report on complete set of FS for audit of SFS or SEFS
Consider implications of certain matters included in $A/R$ on complete set of FS relevant for audit of SFS
or SEFS & it's $A/R$. This involves professional judgment. P.
 Factors that may be relevant in considering those implications include:
Nature of matter(s) described in $A/R$ on complete set of FS & extent to which it relates to SFS/SEFS

10 SA 800 Series
Pervasiveness of matter(s) described in $A/R$ on complete set of FS.
Nature & extent of differences between the AFRFS,
L
F
• Extent of difference between Period(s) covered by complete set of FS compared to period(s) or
of SFS or SEFS.

For eg, if there's qualification of audit opinion for debtors in $A/R$ on complete set of FS & SFS inc
debtors or SEFS relates to debtors, likely that there would be implications for audit.
On other hand, if qualification of opinion on complete set of FS relates to classification of long-
debt, then less likely that there would be implications for audit of SFS i.e. Statement of P&L or if s
relates to debtors.
OM → Inclusion of reference to $A/R$ on complete set of FS (No implications)
Even when matters included in $A/R$ on complete set of FS do not have implications for audit of SF
SEFS, may refer to matter(s) in OM para in $A/R$ on SFS/SEFS as per SA 706.
For eg, may refer in $A/R$ on SFS/SEFS to MURG section included in $A/R$ on complete set of FS.
Adverse or Disclaimer of Opinion in $A/R$ on Complete FS → Then we also have to modify?
If auditor express adverse or disclaim an opinion on entity's complete set of FS, SA 705 doesn't pe
to include in same $A/R$ unmodified opinion on a $\vec{SFS}$ or SEFS. Because such unmodified opinion w
contradict adverse or disclaimer of opinion on entity's complete set of FS.
Exception: If auditor concludes necessary to express adverse or disclaim opinion on entity's comp
set of FS but for separate audit of specific element included in those FS, considers appropriate
express unmodified opinion, auditor shall do so only if:
- Not prohibited by law or regulation
- Opinion is expressed in
$A/R$ Not published with $A/R$ containing adverse or disclaimer of opinion &
Specific element doesn't constitute major portion of entity's complete set of FS.
Q. What about Single Set of FS?
No Exception for single Fis
Not express unmodified opinion on a single FS [if $A/D$ of opinion on Complete FS] even if $A/R$ on sin
FS not published together with $A/R$ containing $A/D$ of opinion.
Because single FS is deemed to constitute a major portion of those FS.
What? It's Different!!
In $A/R$ on entity's complete set of FS, disclaimer of opinion on results of operations & cash flow
unmodified opinion on state of affairs is permitted since disclaimer of opinion is issued for results
operations & cash flows only & not for FS as whole.

contradict adverse or disclaimer of opinion on entity's complete set of FS.
Exception: If auditor concludes necessary to express adverse or disclaim opinion on entity's co
set of FS but for separate audit of specific element included in those FS, considers appropr

Not prohibited by law or regulation

Q. What about Single Set of FS?
No Exception for single Fis
Not express unmodified opinion on a single FS [if $A/D$ of opinion on Complete FS] even if $A/R$ on
FS not published together with $A/R$ containing $A/D$ of opinion.
Because single FS is deemed to constitute a major portion of those FS.
What? It's Different!!
In $A/R$ on entity's complete set of FS, disclaimer of opinion on results of operations & cash f
unmodified opinion on state of affairs is permitted since disclaimer of opinion is issued for resu
operations & cash flows only & not for FS as whole.

10 SA 800 Series
SA 810 - Engg. to Report on Summary FS
What do Summary FS Reflect?
Summary FS reflect historical financial info, derived from FS, but contains less detail than FS while
providing structured representation consistent with FS of entity's economic res resources or obligations at
a point in time or changes therein for a period of time.
Engagement Acceptance
Auditor shall, ordinarily, accept engg. to report on summary FS only when engaged to conduct audit as
per SAs of FS from which summary FS are derived.
If auditor has not audited FS from which summary FS are derived, auditor shall not have necessary
knowledge to discharge responsibilities in relation to summary FS as per this SA.
Before accepting engg. to report on summary FS, auditor shall:
a. Determine whether applied criteria are acceptable.
.
Applied criteria means criteria applied by mat in preparation of summary FS.
Mgt is responsible for determining info. to be reflected in summary FS so that they are consistent, in
all material respects, or represent fair summary of audited FS.
Because summary FS contain aggregated info. & limited disclosure, there is increased risk that they may
be misleading in circumstances.
This risk increases when established criteria for preparation of summary FS do not exist.
Factors affecting auditor's determination of acceptability of applied criteria:
Nature of entity
→ Purpose of the summary FS.
NPIN
→ Info, needs of intended users of summary F.S. &
→ Whether applied criteria will result in summary FS that are not misleading.
☑
Presumption: Criteria for preparation of summary FS may be established by standards setting
organisation or L/R. Auditor may presume that such criteria are acceptable. Where established criteria
don't exist, criteria may be developed by mgt, for eg, based on practice in a particular industry.
If applied criteria is unacceptable:
If auditor concludes applied criteria is unacceptable or unable to obtain agreement of mgt, not accept
engg. to report on summary FS, unless required by $L/R$.
Engg. conducted as per such L/R doesn't comply with this SA.
 A/R on summary FS shall not indicate engg. was conducted as per this SA.
 
 
 
Include appropriate reference to this fact in terms of engg.
Also determine its effect on engg. to audit FS from which summary FS are derived.
Adequate disclosure of summarised nature of summary FS & identity of audited FS be provided by title
such as "Summary FS prepared from audited FS for year ended March 31, 20XX"

Auditor's Report on Summary FS

terms of engg., nature of entity & purpose of summary FS.

Clear expression of opinion.
Statement indicating summary FS don't contain all disclosures required by FRF applied in preparation a

subsequent to date of $A/R$ on audited FS.

When A/R on audited FS contains adverse/disclaimer of opinion, $A/R$ on summary FS shall: -
α. State that $A\overline{A}$ on audited FS contains adverse or disclaimer of opinion

MCQ Modified Opinion on Summary FS
If, summary FS not consistent, in all material respects or not a fair summary of audited FS, as per
applied criteria & mgt doesn't agree to make necessary changes → Adverse Opinion

Auditor engaged to report on FS while not engaged to report on summary FS
If auditor becomes aware that entity plans to make a statement in a document that refers to auditor
fact that summary FS are derived from FS audited by auditor, be satisfied that:
 . Reference to auditor is made in context of $A/R$ on audited FS &
5. Statement doesn't give impression that auditor has reported on summary FS.
Ο If (a) or (b) not met, request mgt to change statement or not refer to auditor in document.
 
Alternatively, entity may engage auditor to report on summary FS & include related $A/R$ in documer
If mgt doesn't agree, advise mgt that auditor disagrees with reference to auditor & take oth
appropriate actions to prevent mgt from inappropriately referring to auditor.
Timing of Work & Events Subsequent to Date of $A/R$ on Audited FS
When $A/R$ on summary FS after completion of audit of FS, auditor not required to obtain add, aud
evidence on audited FS or report on effects of events occurred subsequent to date of $A/R$ on audite
FS since summary FS are derived from audited FS.
Auditor may become aware of facts that existed at date of $A/R$ on audited FS but of which auditor wo
unaware. In such cases, auditor shall not issue $A/R$ on summary FS until auditor's considers such facts
relation to audited FS as per SA 560.
Additional reporting: $A/R$ on summary FS may be dated later than date of $A/R$ on audited FS. In suc
cases, $A/R$ on summary FS shall state that summary FS & audited FS do not reflect effects of event
that occurred subsequent to date of $A/R$ on audited FS.
"Believe you can & you are halfway there"

What is Review?
Ο "Review" is a limited assurance engg.
Limited assurance is obtained where engg. risk is reduced to a level acceptable in circumstances of engg.,
but where risk is greater than reasonable assurance engg.
 Limited assurance engg. provides lower level of assurance than audit.
Involves fewer procedures & SAE on basis of which limited conclusions drawn.
 However, "review" is related to FS prepared on basis of historical financial info. just like audit.
[SAE → Sufficient & Appropriate Evidence]
SRE 2400 Engg. to Review Historical FS.
SRE 2400 deals with: -
☑ Review of historical FS, when practitioner is not auditor of entity's FS &
☑ Form & content of practitioner's report on FS
☐ Notes:
☑ FS may be prepared using general purpose or special purpose framework.
☑ Practitioner's conclusion is based on obtaining limited assurance.
✓ Primarily perform inquiry & analytical procedures to obtain SAE for conclusion on FS.
✓ If practitioner believes FS may be materially misstated, designs & perform additional procedures, to
conclude on FS.
Objectives
Obtain limited assurance via inquiry & analytical procedures about whether FS are free from MM to
express conclusion that FS are prepared in all material respects as per AFRE
Report on FS & communicate as per SRE
Note: If limited assurance can't be obtained & modified conclusion is insufficient, disclaim conclusion o
withdraw if possible under L/R
Q. Roma Ltd entered into contract with Dorma Ltd. There's a condition in contract that Roma Ltd
required to get its FS reviewed for a year on a quarterly basis as per FR provisions of contract.
Can Roma Ltd get its FS reviewed from a professional accountant in practice?
Above FS prepared as per special purpose framework as per requirements of a contract.
FS prepared as per special purpose framework can also be reviewed by a professional accountant
practice & review report may be issued as per SRE 2400.
Compliance with Ethical Requirements & Engagement Level Quality Control
Comply with ethical requirements, including independence & EP is responsible for overall quality of e

Factors affecting Acceptance & Continuance of Client Relationships and Review Engg:
Unless required by L/R, not accept a review engg, if:
Practitioner is Not Satisfied:
11 S
i) That there's a rational purpose for engg. It may be unlikely that there is a rational purpose for en
if, for eg,
1. there is significant limitation on scope of work or
2. suspects association of practitioner's name with FS in inappropriate manner.
3. When engg. is intended to comply with relevant L/R & such L/R requires FS to be audited
ii) Review engg. would be appropriate. When practitioner's preliminary understanding indicat
accepting a review engg. would not be appropriate, may consider recommending another type of eng
Practitioner may believe that audit would be more appropriate. In other cases, if engg. circumstanc
preclude performance of assurance engg, may recommend compilation engg, or other accounting servic
. Reason to believe that relevant Ethical Requirements, including independence, will not be satisfied.
 Preliminary understanding of engg. indicates that INfo, needed is unavailable or unreliable.
. Doubt over mgt's Integrity such is likely to affect proper performance of review
Mgt or TCWG impose a Limitation On Scope that will result in disclaiming a conclusion on FS.
Preconditions for Accepting a Review Engg  ami) Same as SA-210
Prior to accepting a review engg: -
CRC
 Determine whether FRF applied in preparation of FS is acceptable.
including, for special purpose FS, understand purpose for which FS prepared & intended users &
Obtain agreement of mgt that it acknowledges & understands its responsibilities:
i) For preparation of FS as per AFRF, including, fair presentation.
ii) For such IC necessary for preparation of FS free from MM, whether due to fraud/error &
iii) To provide practitioner with: -
AAV
Note:
✓ Access to all info, of which mgt is aware relevant to preparation of FS
Additional info. that practitioner may request from mgt for review &
✓ Unrestricted access to persons within entity necessary to obtain evidence.
If not satisfied with any of above matter before acceptance discuss with mgt/ TCWG.
If changes can't be made to his satisfaction, not accept engg. unless reqd by L/R
What if after acceptance, not satisfied?
Discuss matter with mgt or TCWG and determine:
a. Whether matter can be resolved
b. Whether it's appropriate to continue with engg. &
c. Whether &, if so, how to communicate matter in practitioner's report.

Evidence through inquiry is source of evidence about mgt Intent.
DARAJ
However, info, to support mgt's intent maybe limited. Understanding mgt's Past history, Reasons & Abilit
to pursue specific course of action may provide relevant info.
 Apply professional scepticism in evaluating mgt responses to evaluate whether there's any MM.
 Inquiry also assists in obtaining/updating understanding of entity & its environment, to identify FS arec
where MM likely to arise.

likely to arise.

materially misstated.

115
a. Enable practitioner to conclude that debtors balance is not likely to be materially misstated. In th
case, no further procedures are required.
b. Enable practitioner to determine that matter causes FS to be materially misstated. No furth
procedures are required, & practitioner would form conclusion that FS are materially misstated.

C.

Between Date of FS & Report
Effect of Subsequent Events Hiw
If practitioner becomes aware of events occurring $b/w$ date of FS & practitioner's report that requir
adjustment/disclosure in FS, request mgt to correct those misstatements.
After Report Date
No obligation to perform any procedures regarding FS after date of report.
However, if, after date of report but before date FS are issued, fact becomes known to practitioner
known earlier at date of report, may have caused practitioner to amend report, practitioner shall: -
a. Discuss matter with mgt or TCWG
b. Determine whether FS need amendment &
c. If so, inquire how mgt intends to address the matter in FS
If,
→ mgt doesn't amend FS where practitioner believes they need to be amended, &
→ practitioner's report has already been provided to entity,
notify mgt & TCWG to not issue FS to $3^{rd}$ parties before necessary amendments made.
If FS still issued without necessary amendments, take appropriate action to prevent reliance on repor

After Report Date

known earlier at date of report, may have caused practitioner to amend report, practitioner shall:

Evaluating evidence obtained from procedures performed
Practitioner may not have obtained evidence expected to obtain through inquiry & analytical proce
& procedures addressing specific circumstances.
If practitioner considers that evidence obtained is not SAE to form a conclusion on FS.
Practitioner may:
→ Extend work performed or
→ Perform other necessary procedures.
• Where neither of these is practicable, practitioner will not be able to obtain SAE to form conclus
Eg. if a member of mgt unavailable at time of review to respond to inquiries on significant matter
• If unable to obtain SAE, discuss with mgt & TCWG, effects of limitation on scope on scope of rev

b. Whether FS, including notes, achieve fair presentation or give true & fair view.

With

11
Unmodified Conclusion: Express unmodified conclusion when obtained limited assurance to conclude th
nothing has come to attention that causes to believe that FS not prepared, in all material respects,
per applicable FRF.
When practitioner expresses unmodified conclusion, practitioner shall, unless required by $L/R$, use o
of following phrases, as appropriate: -
a. "Based on our review, nothing has come to our attention that causes us to believe that FS don't give
true & fair view (or don't present fairly, in all material respects), as per AFRF" (fair presentati
framework), or
b. "Based on our review, nothing has come to our attention that causes us to believe that FS are n
prepared, in all material respects, as per AFRF," (compliance framework).
Modified Conclusion: Express modified conclusion when:
a) Based on procedures performed & evidence obtained, FS are materially misstated or
b) Unable to obtain SAAE in relation to 1 or more items in FS that are material in FS as whole.
SAE
When practitioner modifies conclusion expressed on FS, practitioner shall:
a) Use heading "Qualified, Adverse or Disclaimer of Conclusion," as appropriate (Conclusion para) &
b) Provide description of matter giving rise to modification, under appropriate heading (Eg, "Basis fo
Qualified, Adverse or Disclaimer of Conclusion", as appropriate) in a separate para in practitioner
report immediately before-conclusion para (basis for conclusion para).
after
FS are materially misstated
If practitioner determines that FS are materially misstated, express:
a) Qualified conclusion, when effects of matter(s) giving rise to modification are material, but not pervasiv
to FS or
) Adverse conclusion, when effects of matter(s) giving rise to modification are both material & pervasiv
to FS.
Inability to obtain SAAE
If practitioner unable to form conclusion due to inability to obtain SAAE:
) Express qualified conclusion if concludes possible effects on FS of undetected misstatements could b
material but not pervasive or
) Disclaim if concludes possible effects of undetected misstatements could be both material & pervasive
Practitioner shall withdraw from engg. if following conditions present:
 Due to limitation on scope imposed by mgt after engg. is accepted, unable to obtain SAAE to form
conclusion on FS
 Possible effects of undetected misstatements are material & pervasive &
Withdrawal is possible under applicable $L/R$.
